channel.title: AI Security Paper Digest
channel.description: Curated papers on AI security from ArXiv and ACL

item.title: Differentially Private 2D Human Pose Estimation
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces the first comprehensive framework for differentially private 2D human pose estimation using DP-SGD.
  • Proposes Projected DP-SGD (PDP-SGD) for low-dimensional gradient projection, Feature Differential Privacy (FDP) to privatize sensitive features, and a hybrid feature-projective DP approach.
  • Evaluates on the MPII dataset across privacy budgets and hyperparameters, showing the hybrid method outperforms vanilla DP-SGD and achieves up to 82.61% mean PCKh@0.5 at ε=0.8, narrowing the gap to non-private performance.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Kaushik Bhargav Sivangi', 'Paul Henderson', 'Fani Deligianni']
  • Tags: ['differential-privacy', 'privacy-preserving-ml', 'human-pose-estimation', 'DP-SGD', 'computer-vision']

item.title: GAN-Based Single-Stage Defense for Traffic Sign Classification Under Adversarial Patch
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a GAN-based single-stage defense to protect traffic sign classifiers from adversarial patch attacks (APAs) without prior knowledge of patch design or size.
  • Claims computational efficiency suitable for real-time deployment in autonomous vehicles and model-agnostic applicability across traffic sign classifiers.
  • Reports experimental improvements of up to 90% accuracy under APA conditions for considered traffic sign classes and an overall 55% classification accuracy improvement across all tested signs.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Abyad Enan', 'Mashrur Chowdhury']
  • Tags: ['adversarial patch', 'defense', 'GAN', 'autonomous vehicles', 'robustness']

item.title: IoUCert: Robustness Verification for Anchor-based Object Detectors
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces IoUCert, a formal robustness verification framework tailored to anchor-based object detectors (single-object localisation).
  • Proposes a coordinate transformation to avoid relaxation-induced precision loss in non-linear box prediction functions and optimises bounds over anchor box offsets.
  • Develops an Interval Bound Propagation (IBP) variant that computes tight/optimal bounds on Intersection-over-Union (IoU).
  • Demonstrates the method can verify realistic anchor-based models (SSD, YOLOv2, YOLOv3 variants) against input perturbations for the first time.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Benedikt Br\\"uckner', 'Alejandro Mercado', 'Yanghao Zhang', 'Panagiotis Kouvaros', 'Alessio Lomuscio']
  • Tags: ['robustness verification', 'formal verification', 'adversarial robustness', 'object detection', 'IoU']

item.title: MUSE: A Run-Centric Platform for Multimodal Unified Safety Evaluation of Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces MUSE, an open-source, run-centric platform for multimodal safety evaluation and red-teaming of LLMs, integrating cross-modal payload generation, three multi-turn attack algorithms (Crescendo, PAIR, Violent Durian), provider-agnostic model routing, and an LLM judge with a five-level safety taxonomy.
  • Defines a dual-metric framework (hard ASR for strict compliance, soft ASR including partial compliance) to capture partial information leakage that binary metrics miss.
  • Proposes Inter-Turn Modality Switching (ITMS) to test whether alignment generalizes across modalities and shows multi-turn attacks can reach 90–100% ASR even when single-turn refusals are strong; ITMS destabilizes early defenses and its effects are model-family-specific.
  • Evaluates six multimodal LLMs from four providers and demonstrates the need for provider-aware cross-modal safety testing.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zhongxi Wang', 'Yueqian Lin', 'Jingyang Zhang', 'Hai Helen Li', 'Yiran Chen']
  • Tags: ['multimodal-safety', 'red-teaming', 'jailbreaking', 'attack-benchmark', 'safety-evaluation']

item.title: Authenticated Contradictions from Desynchronized Provenance and Watermarking
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces the 'Integrity Clash': assets that pass C2PA provenance checks claiming human authorship while their pixels contain valid AI watermarks, with both verifications succeeding independently.
  • Demonstrates an attack workflow ('metadata washing') that produces such authenticated fakes via standard editing pipelines without breaking cryptography—exploiting a semantic omission allowed by the C2PA spec.
  • Proposes a cross-layer audit protocol that jointly evaluates provenance metadata and watermark detection, achieving 100% classification accuracy on a 3,500-image test set across multiple conflict states and perturbations.
  • Shows the gap between provenance and watermark verification is unnecessary and provides a technically straightforward fix to close the vulnerability.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Alexander Nemecek', 'Hengzhi He', 'Guang Cheng', 'Erman Ayday']
  • Tags: ['C2PA', 'watermarking', 'authenticated fakes', 'metadata washing', 'cross-layer defense']

item.title: MoD-DPO: Towards Mitigating Cross-modal Hallucinations in Omni LLMs using Modality Decoupled Preference Optimization
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes MoD-DPO, a modality-decoupled direct preference optimization method to reduce cross-modal hallucinations in omni LLMs.
  • Introduces modality-aware regularizers that enforce invariance to irrelevant-modality corruptions and sensitivity to relevant-modality perturbations.
  • Adds a language-prior debiasing penalty to discourage text-only, hallucination-prone responses.
  • Demonstrates improved perception accuracy and hallucination resistance on multiple audiovisual benchmarks versus prior preference optimization baselines.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ashutosh Chaubey', 'Jiacheng Pang', 'Mohammad Soleymani']
  • Tags: ['hallucination mitigation', 'multimodal robustness', 'preference optimization', 'debiasing', 'model safety']

item.title: Conditioned Activation Transport for T2I Safety Steering
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Conditioned Activation Transport (CAT), an inference-time defense that conditions nonlinear activation transport maps to trigger only in unsafe activation regions to prevent unsafe/toxic T2I outputs.
  • Introduces SafeSteerDataset, a contrastive dataset of 2300 safe/unsafe prompt pairs to train geometry-based conditioning and reduce interference with benign prompts.
  • Demonstrates effectiveness on two T2I architectures (Z-Image and Infinity), showing reduced attack success rates while preserving image fidelity compared to unsteered or linear steering baselines.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Maciej Chrab\\k{a}szcz', 'Aleksander Szymczyk', "Jan Dubi\\'nski", "Tomasz Trzci\\'nski", 'Franziska Boenisch', 'Adam Dziedzic']
  • Tags: ['Text-to-Image Safety', 'Activation Steering', 'Defense', 'Safety Dataset']

item.title: Mind the Way You Select Negative Texts: Pursuing the Distance Consistency in OOD Detection with VLMs
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes InterNeg, a framework to improve out-of-distribution (OOD) detection for CLIP-like vision-language models by enforcing inter-modal distance consistency when selecting/generating negative texts.
  • Introduces an inter-modal criterion to select negative text candidates and a visual-to-text inversion of high-confidence OOD images to create additional negative text embeddings.
  • Demonstrates state-of-the-art improvements on multiple OOD benchmarks (e.g., reduced FPR95 on ImageNet, improved AUROC on Near-OOD).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zhikang Xu', 'Qianqian Xu', 'Zitai Wang', 'Cong Hua', 'Sicong Li', 'Zhiyong Yang', 'Qingming Huang']
  • Tags: ['OOD detection', 'Vision-Language Models', 'Robustness/Defenses', 'Negative sample selection', 'CLIP']

item.title: AutoFFS: Adversarial Deformations for Facial Feminization Surgery Planning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes AutoFFS, a data-driven framework that generates counterfactual skull morphologies via adversarial free-form deformations for facial feminization surgery planning.
  • Implements a deformation-based targeted adversarial attack on an ensemble of pre-trained binary sex classifiers to transform individual skull shapes toward a target sex.
  • Validates generated morphologies using classifier-based evaluation and a human perceptual study showing they exhibit target-sex characteristics.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Paul Friedrich', 'Florentin Bieder', 'Florian M. Thieringer', 'Philippe C. Cattin']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-examples', 'counterfactual-generation', 'medical-imaging', 'targeted-attacks']

item.title: Spilled Energy in Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Reinterprets the final LLM softmax as an Energy-Based Model (EBM), decomposing sequence generation into interacting EBMs and enabling tracking of 'energy spills' during decoding.
  • Introduces two training-free logits-derived metrics — spilled energy (discrepancy across consecutive steps) and marginalized energy (single-step measurable) — that correlate with factual errors, biases, and hallucinations.
  • Evaluates the metrics across nine benchmarks and several state-of-the-art LLMs (e.g., LLaMA, Mistral, Gemma) and synthetic algebraic tasks, demonstrating robust, cross-task hallucination detection without additional training.
  • Method localizes answer tokens and tests for hallucinations without trained probe classifiers or activation ablations, working for both pretrained and instruction-tuned models.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Adrian Robert Minut', 'Hazem Dewidar', 'Iacopo Masi']
  • Tags: ['hallucination-detection', 'energy-based-models', 'model-robustness', 'interpretability', 'safety-detection']

item.title: Mitigating Over-Refusal in Aligned Large Language Models via Inference-Time Activation Energy
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Energy Landscape Steering (ELS), a fine-tuning-free, inference-time defense that uses an external Energy-Based Model (EBM) to steer LLM hidden states toward desirable behaviors.
  • EBM assigns high energy to undesirable states (false refusals or jailbreaks) and low energy to desirable states (helpful responses or safe rejects); gradients of the energy guide activations in real time.
  • Decouples behavioral control from model weights, enabling computationally efficient intervention without modifying the base model.
  • Empirical results show substantial reduction in over-refusal (e.g., ORB-H compliance from 57.3% to 82.6%) while preserving baseline safety.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Eric Hanchen Jiang', 'Weixuan Ou', 'Run Liu', 'Shengyuan Pang', 'Guancheng Wan', 'Ranjie Duan', 'Wei Dong', 'Kai-Wei Chang', 'XiaoFeng Wang', 'Ying Nian Wu', 'Xinfeng Li']
  • Tags: ['defenses', 'safety-alignment', 'energy-based-model', 'inference-time-mitigation']

item.title: BitBypass: A New Direction in Jailbreaking Aligned Large Language Models with Bitstream Camouflage
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes BitBypass, a novel black-box jailbreak that uses hyphen-separated bitstream camouflage to circumvent safety alignment in LLMs.
  • Demonstrates the attack on multiple state-of-the-art LLMs (GPT-4o, Gemini 1.5, Claude 3.5, Llama 3.1, Mixtral), reporting high success and improved stealthiness versus existing jailbreaks.
  • Shifts the attack surface to fundamental information representation (bit-level encoding) rather than traditional prompt engineering or adversarial text manipulations.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Kalyan Nakka', 'Nitesh Saxena']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'adversarial-attack', 'black-box', 'model-safety', 'prompt-obfuscation']

item.title: Topic-Based Watermarks for Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a lightweight, topic-guided watermarking scheme that partitions vocabulary into topic-aligned token subsets and green-lists tokens relevant to a prompt to embed detectable marks.
  • Design aims to preserve generation quality and fluency while improving robustness of watermarks to paraphrasing and lexical perturbation attacks, without requiring nonstandard generation frameworks.
  • Empirical evaluation across multiple LLMs and benchmarks shows competitive text quality with improved watermark robustness and minimal performance overhead, enabling easier adoption for large-scale watermarking.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Alexander Nemecek', 'Yuzhou Jiang', 'Erman Ayday']
  • Tags: ['watermarking', 'defense', 'robustness', 'LLM detection', 'adversarial resilience']

item.title: AuditBench: Evaluating Alignment Auditing Techniques on Models with Hidden Behaviors
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces AuditBench: 56 language models with 14 types of implanted hidden (non-confessed) behaviors, varying in subtlety and training techniques.
  • Provides an autonomous investigator agent that uses configurable auditing tools to detect hidden behaviors, enabling quantitative evaluation of auditing techniques.
  • Finds a tool-to-agent gap where tools that perform well standalone may not improve an agent's performance; scaffolded auxiliary-model prompt generation and black-box tools worked best, and auditing difficulty varied by training method.
  • Releases models, agent, and evaluation framework to enable iterative research on alignment auditing.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Abhay Sheshadri', 'Aidan Ewart', 'Kai Fronsdal', 'Isha Gupta', 'Samuel R. Bowman', 'Sara Price', 'Samuel Marks', 'Rowan Wang']
  • Tags: ['alignment-auditing', 'red-teaming', 'vulnerability-detection', 'benchmark']

item.title: Steer2Edit: From Activation Steering to Component-Level Editing
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Steer2Edit, a training-free framework that converts inference-time steering vectors into diagnostic signals for component-level rank-1 weight edits.
  • Selective redistribution of behavioral influence across attention heads and MLP neurons yields interpretable edits that preserve the standard forward pass and are compatible with optimized inference.
  • Demonstrates improved attribute-utility trade-offs across safety alignment, hallucination mitigation, and reasoning efficiency (e.g., up to +17.2% safety, +9.8% truthfulness, −12.2% reasoning length on average).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Chung-En Sun', 'Ge Yan', 'Zimo Wang', 'Tsui-Wei Weng']
  • Tags: ['safety-alignment', 'weight-editing', 'activation-steering', 'model-robustness', 'hallucination-mitigation']

item.title: STARS: Synchronous Token Alignment for Robust Supervision in Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces STARS, a decoding-time algorithm that enforces verification at fixed-horizon intervals, decoupling segmentation from model uncertainty to steer generation.
  • Addresses two limitations of uncertainty-based dynamic inference: miscalibrated confident hallucinations and inefficient asynchronous ragged-batch execution, enabling lockstep parallelism and bounded rejection costs.
  • Empirical results on HH-RLHF show STARS matches or exceeds state-of-the-art dynamic inference methods and fine-tuning baselines in alignment quality while improving throughput and system efficiency; code released.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Mohammad Atif Quamar', 'Mohammad Areeb', 'Mikhail Kuznetsov', 'Muslum Ozgur Ozmen', 'Z. Berkay Celik']
  • Tags: ['inference-time defense', 'hallucination detection', 'LLM alignment', 'decoding algorithms', 'robust supervision']

item.title: Reproduction and Replication of an Adversarial Stylometry Experiment
item.description:
Summary

  • Reproduces and replicates Brennan et al. (2012) experiments on defenses against authorship attribution using original data and a repeated online field experiment.
  • Finds the original conclusion largely holds but argues the effectiveness of studied defenses may be overstated due to the original study's lack of a control group.
  • Identifies that an automatic method—round-trip translation—appears to reduce authorship attribution effectiveness and warrants re-examination.
  • Provides an empirical, replication-focused assessment of adversarial stylometry defenses and their real-world efficacy.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Haining Wang', 'Patrick Juola', 'Allen Riddell']
  • Tags: ['adversarial stylometry', 'authorship attribution', 'privacy/deanonymization', 'replication study', 'defenses']

item.title: Understanding and Mitigating Dataset Corruption in LLM Steering
item.description:
Summary

    Paper Type

    • Research Paper

    Additional Information

    • Authors: ['Cullen Anderson', 'Narmeen Oozeer', 'Foad Namjoo', 'Remy Ogasawara', 'Amirali Abdullah', 'Jeff M. Phillips']
    • Tags: ['dataset poisoning', 'robustness', 'contrastive steering', 'defenses', 'adversarial attacks']

item.title: No Memorization, No Detection: Output Distribution-Based Contamination Detection in Small Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes and evaluates CDD (Contamination Detection via output Distribution), which detects training-data contamination by measuring the peakedness of a model's sampled outputs.
  • Runs controlled contamination experiments on small LMs (70M–410M) across GSM8K, HumanEval, and MATH, showing CDD succeeds only when fine-tuning induces verbatim memorization.
  • Shows parameter-efficient fine-tuning (e.g., low-rank adaptation) can introduce contamination without memorization, causing CDD to perform at chance despite verifiable contamination.
  • Characterizes a memorization threshold that governs detectability and warns that output-distribution-based detection can miss contamination in PEFT settings.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Omer Sela']
  • Tags: ['data contamination', 'memorization', 'detection', 'model auditing', 'parameter-efficient fine-tuning']

item.title: MoD-DPO: Towards Mitigating Cross-modal Hallucinations in Omni LLMs using Modality Decoupled Preference Optimization
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes MoD-DPO, a modality-decoupled direct preference optimization method to reduce cross-modal hallucinations in omni LLMs.
  • Introduces modality-aware regularizers that enforce invariance to irrelevant-modality corruptions and sensitivity to relevant-modality perturbations.
  • Adds a language-prior debiasing penalty to discourage text-only, hallucination-prone responses.
  • Demonstrates improved perception accuracy and hallucination resistance on multiple audiovisual benchmarks versus prior preference optimization baselines.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ashutosh Chaubey', 'Jiacheng Pang', 'Mohammad Soleymani']
  • Tags: ['hallucination mitigation', 'multimodal robustness', 'preference optimization', 'debiasing', 'model safety']

item.title: MUSE: A Run-Centric Platform for Multimodal Unified Safety Evaluation of Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces MUSE, an open-source, run-centric platform for multimodal safety evaluation and red-teaming of LLMs, integrating cross-modal payload generation, three multi-turn attack algorithms (Crescendo, PAIR, Violent Durian), provider-agnostic model routing, and an LLM judge with a five-level safety taxonomy.
  • Defines a dual-metric framework (hard ASR for strict compliance, soft ASR including partial compliance) to capture partial information leakage that binary metrics miss.
  • Proposes Inter-Turn Modality Switching (ITMS) to test whether alignment generalizes across modalities and shows multi-turn attacks can reach 90–100% ASR even when single-turn refusals are strong; ITMS destabilizes early defenses and its effects are model-family-specific.
  • Evaluates six multimodal LLMs from four providers and demonstrates the need for provider-aware cross-modal safety testing.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zhongxi Wang', 'Yueqian Lin', 'Jingyang Zhang', 'Hai Helen Li', 'Yiran Chen']
  • Tags: ['multimodal-safety', 'red-teaming', 'jailbreaking', 'attack-benchmark', 'safety-evaluation']

item.title: Learning When to Act or Refuse: Guarding Agentic Reasoning Models for Safe Multi-Step Tool Use
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces MOSAIC, a post-training framework that aligns agentic models for safe multi-step tool use by structuring inference as a plan → check → act-or-refuse loop with explicit safety reasoning and refusal actions.
  • Trains safety behavior without trajectory-level labels using preference-based reinforcement learning over pairwise trajectory comparisons to better capture safety distinctions than scalar rewards.
  • Evaluates zero-shot across multiple model families and out-of-distribution benchmarks (harmful tasks, prompt injection, benign tool use, cross-domain privacy leakage), showing substantial reductions in harmful behavior, increased refusal rates on attacks, reduced privacy leakage, and preserved or improved benign performance.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Aradhye Agarwal', 'Gurdit Siyan', 'Yash Pandya', 'Joykirat Singh', 'Akshay Nambi', 'Ahmed Awadallah']
  • Tags: ['agentic-safety', 'tool-use-defense', 'prompt-injection', 'preference-RL', 'privacy-leakage']

item.title: TAO-Attack: Toward Advanced Optimization-Based Jailbreak Attacks for Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes TAO-Attack, an optimization-based jailbreak method for LLMs using a two-stage loss: first to suppress refusals and then to penalize pseudo-harmful outputs and push toward more harmful completions.
  • Introduces Direction-Priority Token Optimization (DPTO), which prioritizes candidate tokens aligned with the gradient direction before considering update magnitude to improve token-level update efficiency.
  • Reports extensive experiments across multiple LLMs showing TAO-Attack outperforms prior methods, achieving higher attack success rates and reaching 100% in some scenarios.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zhi Xu', 'Jiaqi Li', 'Xiaotong Zhang', 'Hong Yu', 'Han Liu']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'adversarial-attacks', 'optimization-based-attacks', 'LLM-security', 'red-teaming']

item.title: PrivMedChat: End-to-End Differentially Private RLHF for Medical Dialogue Systems
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes PrivMedChat, an end-to-end differentially private RLHF (DP-RLHF) framework for medical dialogue that enforces DP via DP-SGD during supervised fine-tuning, reward model training, and PPO actor/critic updates (reward model fixed after DP training).
  • Introduces an annotation-free preference construction method pairing physician responses with filtered non-expert generations to create scalable preference data without clinician labeling.
  • Empirical results show strong utility-privacy trade-offs (e.g., ε=7 achieves highest ROUGE-L among DP models, low clinical hallucination and harmful-advice rates, high LLM-jury score) and membership-inference signals near chance (AUC 0.510–0.555).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Sudip Bhujel']
  • Tags: ['differential privacy', 'privacy-preserving ML', 'RLHF', 'membership inference', 'medical NLP']

item.title: A Browser-based Open Source Assistant for Multimodal Content Verification
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents VERIFICATION ASSISTANT, a browser-based, open-source tool integrated into a popular plugin to help journalists and fact-checkers verify URLs and media files.
  • Automatically extracts content and routes it to multiple backend NLP classifiers to surface credibility signals, estimate AI-generated content, and provide verification guidance.
  • Focuses on accessibility and real-world deployment, demonstrating system architecture and integration of multimodal NLP services for disinformation detection.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Rosanna Milner', 'Michael Foster', 'Olesya Razuvayevskaya', 'Ian Roberts', 'Valentin Porcellini', 'Denis Teyssou', 'Kalina Bontcheva']
  • Tags: ['disinformation-detection', 'ai-generated-content-detection', 'fact-checking-tools', 'browser-extension', 'multimodal-verification']

item.title: ExpGuard: LLM Content Moderation in Specialized Domains
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces ExpGuard, a specialized guardrail model for moderating LLM inputs/outputs in finance, medical, and legal domains.
  • Releases ExpGuardMix, a 58,928-example dataset (ExpGuardTrain and expert-annotated ExpGuardTest) of prompts paired with refusal and compliant responses for training and robust evaluation.
  • Evaluates robustness against domain-specific adversarial attacks and reports improvements over prior state-of-the-art (e.g., WildGuard) in prompt and response classification.
  • Open-sources code, data, and model to enable adaptation to additional domains and further research into robust guardrails.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Minseok Choi', 'Dongjin Kim', 'Seungbin Yang', 'Subin Kim', 'Youngjun Kwak', 'Juyoung Oh', 'Jaegul Choo', 'Jungmin Son']
  • Tags: ['content-moderation', 'guardrails', 'adversarial-robustness', 'dataset', 'domain-specific-safety']

item.title: Characterizing Memorization in Diffusion Language Models: Generalized Extraction and Sampling Effects
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents a theoretical and empirical study of memorization in diffusion language models (DLMs), introducing a generalized probabilistic extraction framework that unifies prefix-conditioned decoding and diffusion-based generation.
  • Proves (Theorem 4.3) that higher sampling resolution strictly increases the probability of exact training-data extraction, showing autoregressive decoding as a limiting high-resolution case.
  • Empirically validates theoretical predictions across model scales and sampling strategies and shows DLMs leak substantially less PII via memorization under aligned prefix-conditioned evaluations compared to autoregressive models.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xiaoyu Luo', 'Wenrui Yu', 'Qiongxiu Li', 'Johannes Bjerva']
  • Tags: ['memorization', 'training-data-extraction', 'privacy-leakage', 'diffusion-language-models', 'theoretical-analysis']

item.title: Linear Model Extraction via Factual and Counterfactual Queries
item.description:
Summary

  • Analyzes model extraction attacks on linear classifiers using three query types: factual, counterfactual, and robust counterfactual.
  • Derives mathematical characterizations of classification regions obtainable from arbitrary query sets without recovering model parameters.
  • Proves bounds on the number of queries needed to recover model parameters: a single counterfactual query suffices with differentiable distance measures; polyhedral distances require O(d) queries, and robust counterfactuals double that requirement.
  • Highlights that the choice of distance function and robustness constraints for counterfactuals materially impact the security (susceptibility to extraction) of linear models.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Daan Otto', 'Jannis Kurtz', 'Dick den Hertog', 'Ilker Birbil']
  • Tags: ['model-extraction', 'model-stealing', 'counterfactual-queries', 'privacy', 'theoretical-security']

item.title: Multimodal Multi-Agent Ransomware Analysis Using AutoGen
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a multimodal, multi-agent framework for ransomware classification that fuses static, dynamic, and network telemetry via specialized agents.
  • Each agent uses autoencoder-based feature extraction; a fusion agent combines representations and a transformer-based classifier predicts ransomware family.
  • Introduces an inter-agent feedback mechanism that iteratively refines representations by suppressing low-confidence signals and supports confidence-aware abstention for safer deployments.
  • Evaluated on large ransomware/benign datasets, reports strong gains over single-modality and nonadaptive fusion baselines (Macro-F1 ≈ 0.936), improved calibration, and stable agent convergence; zero-day detection remains challenging.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Asifullah Khan', 'Aimen Wadood', 'Mubashar Iqbal', 'Umme Zahoora']
  • Tags: ['ransomware detection', 'multimodal fusion', 'multi-agent systems', 'malware classification', 'defensive AI']

item.title: LiteLMGuard: Seamless and Lightweight On-Device Prompt Filtering for Safeguarding Small Language Models against Quantization-induced Risks and Vulnerabilities
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies safety and privacy risks introduced by quantization of Small Language Models (SLMs) for on-device deployment, which can cause models to respond directly to harmful queries (including jailbreaks).
  • Proposes LiteLMGuard, a lightweight, model-agnostic on-device DL-based prompt filter that classifies prompt 'answerability' to block harmful or unsafe prompts before they reach quantized SLMs.
  • Implements an ELECTRA-based classifier trained on a curated Answerable-or-Not dataset, achieving 97.75% classification accuracy and real-time on-device performance (~135 ms latency) with >85% defense-rate and 94% filtering accuracy.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Kalyan Nakka', 'Jimmy Dani', 'Ausmit Mondal', 'Nitesh Saxena']
  • Tags: ['on-device security', 'prompt filtering', 'jailbreak defense', 'quantization risks', 'LLM safety']

item.title: Topic-Based Watermarks for Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a lightweight, topic-guided watermarking scheme that partitions vocabulary into topic-aligned token subsets and green-lists tokens relevant to a prompt to embed detectable marks.
  • Design aims to preserve generation quality and fluency while improving robustness of watermarks to paraphrasing and lexical perturbation attacks, without requiring nonstandard generation frameworks.
  • Empirical evaluation across multiple LLMs and benchmarks shows competitive text quality with improved watermark robustness and minimal performance overhead, enabling easier adoption for large-scale watermarking.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Alexander Nemecek', 'Yuzhou Jiang', 'Erman Ayday']
  • Tags: ['watermarking', 'defense', 'robustness', 'LLM detection', 'adversarial resilience']

item.title: FlexGuard: Continuous Risk Scoring for Strictness-Adaptive LLM Content Moderation
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces FlexBench, a strictness-adaptive moderation benchmark to evaluate moderators under multiple enforcement strictness regimes.
  • Shows existing binary moderators suffer substantial cross-strictness inconsistency—models that work under one regime degrade under others.
  • Proposes FlexGuard, an LLM-based moderator that produces a calibrated continuous risk score trained via risk-alignment optimization to reflect severity and enable thresholding for different strictness levels.
  • Provides threshold selection strategies and demonstrates improved moderation accuracy and robustness across strictness regimes on FlexBench and public benchmarks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zhihao Ding', 'Jinming Li', 'Ze Lu', 'Jieming Shi']
  • Tags: ['content-moderation', 'safety-guardrails', 'risk-scoring', 'robustness', 'benchmarking']

item.title: Learning Contextual Runtime Monitors for Safe AI-Based Autonomy
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a framework to learn context-aware runtime monitors that select the most appropriate controller from an ensemble based on current operating context.
  • Frames monitor learning as a contextual multi-armed bandit problem, providing theoretical safety guarantees for controller selection.
  • Demonstrates improved safety and performance over non-contextual baselines in simulated autonomous driving scenarios.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Alejandro Luque-Cerpa', 'Mengyuan Wang', 'Emil Carlsson', 'Sanjit A. Seshia', 'Devdatt Dubhashi', 'Hazem Torfah']
  • Tags: ['runtime monitoring', 'safety mechanisms', 'contextual bandits', 'autonomous systems']

item.title: Dual Randomized Smoothing: Beyond Global Noise Variance
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Dual Randomized Smoothing (Dual RS), which uses input-dependent noise variances to overcome the limitations of a single global noise variance in standard randomized smoothing.
  • Proves RS validity with input-dependent variances under a local-constancy condition and smooths the variance estimator itself to satisfy this requirement.
  • Designs a two-component system (a variance estimator and a standard RS classifier), training strategies to jointly optimize them, and demonstrates improved certified accuracy across small and large radii.
  • Empirical gains on CIFAR-10 and ImageNet (notable percentage improvements at various radii) with ~60% additional inference cost and compatibility with ensembles/expert RS models.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Chenhao Sun', 'Yuhao Mao', 'Martin Vechev']
  • Tags: ['adversarial_robustness', 'randomized_smoothing', 'certified_defense', 'input_dependent_noise', 'robustness_benchmarking']

item.title: WARP: Weight Teleportation for Attack-Resilient Unlearning Protocols
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies privacy vulnerabilities in approximate machine unlearning, attributing leakage to large forget-set gradient norms and close proximity of unlearned parameters to the original model.
  • Proposes unlearning-specific membership inference and reconstruction attacks and demonstrates state-of-the-art unlearning methods remain vulnerable.
  • Introduces WARP, a plug-and-play 'weight teleportation' defense that leverages neural network symmetries to reduce forget-set gradient energy and increase parameter dispersion while preserving utility.
  • Reports strong empirical results across six unlearning algorithms: up to 64% reduction in adversarial AUC (black-box) and up to 92% (white-box) while maintaining retained-data accuracy.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Mohammad M Maheri', 'Xavier Cadet', 'Peter Chin', 'Hamed Haddadi']
  • Tags: ['membership-inference', 'model-reconstruction', 'machine-unlearning', 'defense']

item.title: Mitigating Over-Refusal in Aligned Large Language Models via Inference-Time Activation Energy
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Energy Landscape Steering (ELS), a fine-tuning-free, inference-time defense that uses an external Energy-Based Model (EBM) to steer LLM hidden states toward desirable behaviors.
  • EBM assigns high energy to undesirable states (false refusals or jailbreaks) and low energy to desirable states (helpful responses or safe rejects); gradients of the energy guide activations in real time.
  • Decouples behavioral control from model weights, enabling computationally efficient intervention without modifying the base model.
  • Empirical results show substantial reduction in over-refusal (e.g., ORB-H compliance from 57.3% to 82.6%) while preserving baseline safety.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Eric Hanchen Jiang', 'Weixuan Ou', 'Run Liu', 'Shengyuan Pang', 'Guancheng Wan', 'Ranjie Duan', 'Wei Dong', 'Kai-Wei Chang', 'XiaoFeng Wang', 'Ying Nian Wu', 'Xinfeng Li']
  • Tags: ['defenses', 'safety-alignment', 'energy-based-model', 'inference-time-mitigation']

item.title: Adversarial Attacks in Weight-Space Classifiers
item.description:
Summary

  • Examines security of weight-space (parameter-space) classifiers built from Implicit Neural Representations (INRs), focusing on adversarial robustness.
  • Finds parameter-space classifiers appear more robust to standard white-box adversarial attacks without robust training, attributing this to gradient obfuscation arising during INR optimization.
  • Develops a novel suite of adversarial attacks specifically targeting parameter-space classifiers and analyzes practical limitations and failure cases of the observed robustness.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Tamir Shor', 'Ethan Fetaya', 'Chaim Baskin', 'Alex Bronstein']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-examples', 'model-robustness', 'gradient-obfuscation', 'parameter-space-attacks', 'implicit-neural-representations']

item.title: Few-shot Model Extraction Attacks against Sequential Recommender Systems
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a few-shot model extraction attack framework targeting sequential recommender systems, where adversaries have access to limited raw data (≤10%).
  • Introduces an autoregressive augmentation generation strategy to synthesize data that mimics user interaction distributions.
  • Designs a bidirectional repair loss during distillation to align recommendation lists and correct surrogate model errors by transferring knowledge from the victim model.
  • Evaluates the approach on three datasets and reports that the framework yields more effective surrogate models under few-shot conditions.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Hui Zhang', 'Fu Liu']
  • Tags: ['model-extraction', 'few-shot', 'recommender-systems', 'black-box-attack', 'data-augmentation']

item.title: MoD-DPO: Towards Mitigating Cross-modal Hallucinations in Omni LLMs using Modality Decoupled Preference Optimization
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes MoD-DPO, a modality-decoupled direct preference optimization method to reduce cross-modal hallucinations in omni LLMs.
  • Introduces modality-aware regularizers that enforce invariance to irrelevant-modality corruptions and sensitivity to relevant-modality perturbations.
  • Adds a language-prior debiasing penalty to discourage text-only, hallucination-prone responses.
  • Demonstrates improved perception accuracy and hallucination resistance on multiple audiovisual benchmarks versus prior preference optimization baselines.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ashutosh Chaubey', 'Jiacheng Pang', 'Mohammad Soleymani']
  • Tags: ['hallucination mitigation', 'multimodal robustness', 'preference optimization', 'debiasing', 'model safety']

item.title: Silent Sabotage During Fine-Tuning: Few-Shot Rationale Poisoning of Compact Medical LLMs
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a novel poisoning attack—few-shot rationale poisoning—targeting the supervised fine-tuning (SFT) stage of medical LLMs to stealthily degrade reasoning on specific medical topics.
  • Finds that injecting poisoned rationales (rather than overwriting factual knowledge) causes significant accuracy drops on targeted subjects, provided no correct samples of that subject are present in the dataset.
  • Characterizes attack efficiency: a minimum number and ratio of poisoned samples are required for an effective, stealthy attack, and rationale poisoning outperforms catastrophic forgetting/knowledge overwriting in this setting.
  • Demonstrates a realistic SFT-stage vulnerability in sensitive medical domains and calls for development of defenses against such stealthy poisoning.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jingyuan Xie', 'Wenjie Wang', 'Ji Wu', 'Jiandong Gao']
  • Tags: ['data poisoning', 'fine-tuning attacks', 'rationale poisoning', 'medical LLMs', 'stealthy adversarial attacks']

item.title: The Alignment Flywheel: A Governance-Centric Hybrid MAS for Architecture-Agnostic Safety
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes the Alignment Flywheel, a governance-centric hybrid multi-agent system architecture that decouples decision generation (Proposer) from safety governance (Safety Oracle and enforcement layer).
  • Specifies runtime gating, explicit risk policy enforcement, audit intake, uncertainty-driven verification, versioned refinement, signed patching, and staged rollout to enable patch-locality fixes without retraining decision components.
  • Focuses on implementation-agnostic roles, artifacts, protocols, and release semantics to provide auditable, version-controlled oversight of fallible autonomous components.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ["Elias Malomgr\\'e", 'Pieter Simoens']
  • Tags: ['safety mechanisms', 'runtime enforcement', 'governance & auditing', 'patching/versioning']

item.title: Understanding and Mitigating Dataset Corruption in LLM Steering
item.description:
Summary

    Paper Type

    • Research Paper

    Additional Information

    • Authors: ['Cullen Anderson', 'Narmeen Oozeer', 'Foad Namjoo', 'Remy Ogasawara', 'Amirali Abdullah', 'Jeff M. Phillips']
    • Tags: ['dataset poisoning', 'robustness', 'contrastive steering', 'defenses', 'adversarial attacks']

item.title: IoUCert: Robustness Verification for Anchor-based Object Detectors
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces IoUCert, a formal robustness verification framework tailored to anchor-based object detectors (single-object localisation).
  • Proposes a coordinate transformation to avoid relaxation-induced precision loss in non-linear box prediction functions and optimises bounds over anchor box offsets.
  • Develops an Interval Bound Propagation (IBP) variant that computes tight/optimal bounds on Intersection-over-Union (IoU).
  • Demonstrates the method can verify realistic anchor-based models (SSD, YOLOv2, YOLOv3 variants) against input perturbations for the first time.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Benedikt Br\\"uckner', 'Alejandro Mercado', 'Yanghao Zhang', 'Panagiotis Kouvaros', 'Alessio Lomuscio']
  • Tags: ['robustness verification', 'formal verification', 'adversarial robustness', 'object detection', 'IoU']

item.title: Why Does RLAIF Work At All?
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes the latent value hypothesis: pretraining encodes human values as directions in representation space and constitutional prompts/projectors elicit those latent values into preference judgments.
  • Formalizes RLAIF with a linear model showing when RLAIF improves alignment, how the ceiling of improvement depends on how well values are encoded (scaling with model capacity), and explains the generation–judgment gap.
  • Unifies empirical observations (refusal direction, low-rank safety subspaces, RLAIF scaling) and highlights security risks by demonstrating that adversarial constitutions can activate anti-social value directions learned from harmful pretraining data.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Robin Young']
  • Tags: ['RLAIF', 'alignment', 'adversarial constitutions / jailbreaking', 'representation-based safety', 'theoretical analysis']

item.title: From Shallow to Deep: Pinning Semantic Intent via Causal GRPO
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies a vulnerability called 'Shallow Safety Alignment' where LLMs' internal malicious intent signal decays as they generate compliant prefixes, enabling adversarial prefix/jailbreak attacks.
  • Proposes Two-Stage Causal-GRPO (TSC-GRPO): (1) train a causal intent probe to disentangle invariant intent from stylistic perturbations, and (2) internalize this via Group Relative Policy Optimization with a cumulative causal penalty in 'fork-in-the-road' training scenarios.
  • Shows experimental results where TSC-GRPO improves robustness to jailbreak/adversarial prefix attacks while preserving general utility compared to baselines.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Shuyi Zhou', 'Zeen Song', 'Wenwen Qiang', 'Jiyan Sun', 'Yao Zhou', 'Yinlong Liu', 'Wei Ma']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'adversarial prefix attacks', 'defense', 'causal probing', 'policy optimization']

item.title: SaFeR-ToolKit: Structured Reasoning via Virtual Tool Calling for Multimodal Safety
item.description:
Summary

  • Formalizes multimodal safety decision-making as a checkable tool-using protocol (planner defines persona and Perception→Reasoning→Decision tools; responder emits a typed tool trace before final answer).
  • Proposes a three-stage training curriculum (SFT → DPO → GRPO) where GRPO supervises tool usage beyond answer-level feedback to enforce protocol compliance.
  • Releases the first tool-based safety reasoning dataset (31,654 examples + 1k held-out) and evaluates on Qwen2.5-VL, showing large improvements in safety, helpfulness, and reasoning rigor while preserving capabilities.
  • Addresses defenses against multimodal jailbreaks and over-refusal by providing a structured, checkable pipeline and training procedure to improve guardrails.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zixuan Xu', 'Tiancheng He', 'Huahui Yi', 'Kun Wang', 'Xi Chen', 'Gongli Xi', 'Qiankun Li', 'Kang Li', 'Yang Liu', 'Zhigang Zeng']
  • Tags: ['multimodal safety', 'jailbreak defense', 'tool-based safety protocol', 'safety dataset', 'alignment training']

item.title: MUSE: A Run-Centric Platform for Multimodal Unified Safety Evaluation of Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces MUSE, an open-source, run-centric platform for multimodal safety evaluation and red-teaming of LLMs, integrating cross-modal payload generation, three multi-turn attack algorithms (Crescendo, PAIR, Violent Durian), provider-agnostic model routing, and an LLM judge with a five-level safety taxonomy.
  • Defines a dual-metric framework (hard ASR for strict compliance, soft ASR including partial compliance) to capture partial information leakage that binary metrics miss.
  • Proposes Inter-Turn Modality Switching (ITMS) to test whether alignment generalizes across modalities and shows multi-turn attacks can reach 90–100% ASR even when single-turn refusals are strong; ITMS destabilizes early defenses and its effects are model-family-specific.
  • Evaluates six multimodal LLMs from four providers and demonstrates the need for provider-aware cross-modal safety testing.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zhongxi Wang', 'Yueqian Lin', 'Jingyang Zhang', 'Hai Helen Li', 'Yiran Chen']
  • Tags: ['multimodal-safety', 'red-teaming', 'jailbreaking', 'attack-benchmark', 'safety-evaluation']

item.title: NExT-Guard: Training-Free Streaming Safeguard without Token-Level Labels
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes NExT-Guard, a training-free framework for real-time (streaming) safety that monitors latent features to intercept unsafe content before token emission.
  • Uses Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) pretrained from base LLMs to extract interpretable token-level risk signals without requiring token-level supervised labels.
  • Claims superior performance and robustness compared to both post-hoc and supervised streaming safeguards across models, SAE variants, and risk scenarios.
  • Targets practical, low-cost deployment of streaming guardrails for LLMs by avoiding expensive annotations and reducing overfitting associated with supervised token-level training.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Junfeng Fang', 'Nachuan Chen', 'Houcheng Jiang', 'Dan Zhang', 'Fei Shen', 'Xiang Wang', 'Xiangnan He', 'Tat-Seng Chua']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'streaming safeguards', 'LLM safety', 'sparse autoencoders', 'training-free safety']

item.title: Jailbreak Foundry: From Papers to Runnable Attacks for Reproducible Benchmarking
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces JAILBREAK FOUNDRY (JBF), a system that converts jailbreak attack papers into runnable modules and evaluates them in a unified harness.
  • Contains three components: JBF-LIB (shared utilities), JBF-FORGE (multi-agent paper-to-module translation), and JBF-EVAL (standardized evaluations with a consistent judge).
  • Reproduced 30 attacks with high fidelity (mean ASR deviation +0.26 percentage points), reduces per-attack implementation effort, and enables standardized AdvBench evaluations across multiple victim models.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zhicheng Fang', 'Jingjie Zheng', 'Chenxu Fu', 'Wei Xu']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'reproducibility', 'adversarial-evaluation', 'benchmarking', 'red-teaming']

item.title: Agentic Unlearning: When LLM Agent Meets Machine Unlearning
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces "agentic unlearning" to remove specified information from both model parameters and persistent agent memory in closed-loop LLM agents.
  • Identifies two gaps in prior unlearning: parameter-memory backflow and lack of unified cross-pathway strategies.
  • Proposes Synchronized Backflow Unlearning (SBU): dependency-closure-based memory pruning + stochastic reference alignment for parametric suppression, integrated via a synchronized dual-update protocol.
  • Evaluated on medical QA tasks, showing reduced traces of targeted private information across both pathways with limited utility degradation.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Bin Wang', 'Fan Wang', 'Pingping Wang', 'Jinyu Cong', 'Yang Yu', 'Yilong Yin', 'Zhongyi Han', 'Benzheng Wei']
  • Tags: ['machine-unlearning', 'privacy-preservation', 'LLM-agents', 'defense', 'memory-management']

item.title: Trojans in Artificial Intelligence (TrojAI) Final Report
item.description:
Summary

  • Final report from IARPA's TrojAI program addressing AI Trojans (malicious backdoors) including threat mapping, detection methods, and mitigation strategies.
  • Describes detection methodologies such as weight analysis and trigger inversion, and provides comprehensive test & evaluation results on detector performance and sensitivity.
  • Documents prevalence of naturally occurring Trojans, lessons learned, unsolved challenges, and recommendations for advancing AI security research and deployment defenses.

Paper Type

  • Survey

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Kristopher W. Reese', 'Taylor Kulp-McDowall', 'Michael Majurski', 'Tim Blattner', 'Derek Juba', 'Peter Bajcsy', 'Antonio Cardone', 'Philippe Dessauw', 'Alden Dima', 'Anthony J. Kearsley', 'Melinda Kleczynski', 'Joel Vasanth', 'Walid Keyrouz', 'Chace Ashcraft', 'Neil Fendley', 'Ted Staley', 'Trevor Stout', 'Josh Carney', 'Greg Canal', 'Will Redman', 'Aurora Schmidt', 'Cameron Hickert', 'William Paul', 'Jared Markowitz', 'Nathan Drenkow', 'David Shriver', 'Marissa Connor', 'Keltin Grimes', 'Marco Christiani', 'Hayden Moore', 'Jordan Widjaja', 'Kasimir Gabert', 'Uma Balakrishnan', 'Satyanadh Gundimada', 'John Jacobellis', 'Sandya Lakkur', 'Vitus Leung', 'Jon Roose', 'Casey Battaglino', 'Farinaz Koushanfar', 'Greg Fields', 'Xihe Gu', 'Yaman Jandali', 'Xinqiao Zhang', 'Tara Javidi', 'Akash Vartak', 'Tim Oates', 'Ben Erichson', 'Michael Mahoney', 'Rauf Izmailov', 'Xiangyu Zhang', 'Guangyu Shen', 'Siyuan Cheng', 'Shiqing Ma', 'XiaoFeng Wang', 'Haixu Tang', 'Di Tang', 'Xiaoyi Chen', 'Zihao Wang', 'Rui Zhu', 'Susmit Jha', 'Xiao Lin', 'Manoj Acharya', 'Weichao Zhou', 'Feisi Fu', 'Panagiota Kiourti', 'Chenyu Wang', 'Zijian Guo', 'H M Sabbir Ahmad', 'Wenchao Li', 'Chao Chen']
  • Tags: ['trojan/backdoor', 'backdoor detection', 'trigger inversion', 'mitigation', 'benchmarking/evaluation']

item.title: Stealthy Poisoning Attacks Bypass Defenses in Regression Settings
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes an optimal stealthy poisoning attack formulation for regression models that accounts for varying degrees of detectability and demonstrates it can bypass state-of-the-art defenses.
  • Introduces a normalization-based methodology to evaluate trade-offs between attack effectiveness and detectability.
  • Presents a novel defense, BayesClean, which improves robustness against stealthy poisoning when the number of poisoning points is significant.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Javier Carnerero-Cano', "Luis Mu\\~noz-Gonz\\'alez", 'Phillippa Spencer', 'Emil C. Lupu']
  • Tags: ['data-poisoning', 'poisoning-attacks', 'regression-robustness', 'defenses', 'adversarial-ml']

item.title: Learn-to-Distance: Distance Learning for Detecting LLM-Generated Text
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents a geometric interpretation of rewrite-based detection methods for identifying LLM-generated text and motivates adaptive distance learning.
  • Proposes a novel rewrite-based detector that learns an adaptive distance function between original and rewritten text, with theoretical guarantees that learned distances outperform fixed ones.
  • Extensive empirical evaluation (100+ settings) showing substantial relative improvements (54.3%–75.4%) over strong baselines across multiple target LLMs (GPT, Claude, Gemini).
  • Provides an open-source Python implementation.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Hongyi Zhou', 'Jin Zhu', 'Kai Ye', 'Ying Yang', 'Erhan Xu', 'Chengchun Shi']
  • Tags: ['LLM-generated-text-detection', 'rewrite-based-detection', 'distance-learning', 'text-forensics', 'defense']

item.title: GNN Explanations that do not Explain and How to find Them
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies a critical failure mode in self-explainable GNNs (SE-GNNs): explanations can be unambiguously unrelated to the model's true inference process (degenerate explanations).
  • Shows SE-GNNs can attain optimal predictive performance while producing degenerate explanations, and common faithfulness metrics often fail to detect these failures.
  • Demonstrates degenerate explanations can be maliciously planted to hide use of sensitive attributes (attack scenario) and can also arise naturally, motivating the need for reliable auditing.
  • Proposes a novel faithfulness metric that reliably flags degenerate explanations as unfaithful in both adversarial and natural settings.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Steve Azzolin', 'Stefano Teso', 'Bruno Lepri', 'Andrea Passerini', 'Sagar Malhotra']
  • Tags: ['explainability', 'graph neural networks', 'adversarial manipulation', 'audit/defense', 'privacy']

item.title: HalluGuard: Demystifying Data-Driven and Reasoning-Driven Hallucinations in LLMs
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a theoretical Hallucination Risk Bound that decomposes LLM hallucinations into data-driven (training-time mismatches) and reasoning-driven (inference-time instabilities) components.
  • Proposes HalluGuard, an NTK-based scoring method that leverages induced geometry/representations to jointly detect data- and reasoning-driven hallucinations.
  • Evaluates HalluGuard across 10 benchmarks, 11 baselines, and 9 LLM backbones, reporting state-of-the-art hallucination detection; code is open-sourced.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xinyue Zeng', 'Junhong Lin', 'Yujun Yan', 'Feng Guo', 'Liang Shi', 'Jun Wu', 'Dawei Zhou']
  • Tags: ['hallucination detection', 'LLM safety', 'Neural Tangent Kernel (NTK)', 'robustness', 'reliability']

item.title: Towards Transferable Defense Against Malicious Image Edits
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes TDAE, a bimodal (image+text) defense framework to make images immune to malicious edits in diffusion-based image editors by coordinated image and text optimization.
  • Introduces FlatGrad Defense Mechanism (FDM): gradient-regularized adversarial perturbations aimed at steering defenses toward flat minima for improved cross-model robustness.
  • Introduces Dynamic Prompt Defense (DPD): iterative adversarial refinement of text embeddings to align edited outputs of immunized images with originals, increasing transferability across unseen editing models.
  • Reports state-of-the-art mitigation of malicious image edits in both intra-model and cross-model evaluations, emphasizing transferability of defenses.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jie Zhang', 'Shuai Dong', 'Shiguang Shan', 'Xilin Chen']
  • Tags: ['adversarial defense', 'image editing', 'diffusion models', 'transferability', 'prompt-based defense']

item.title: AudAgent: Automated Auditing of Privacy Policy Compliance in AI Agents
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents AudAgent, a system for real-time automated auditing of AI agents to verify compliance with stated privacy policies.
  • Combines policy formalization (cross-LLM voting), runtime sensitive-data annotation (Presidio-based), and automata/ontology-based compliance checking to detect violations on-the-fly.
  • Provides an infrastructure-independent UI that visualizes execution traces and detected privacy breaches and can proactively block operations on highly sensitive data.
  • Evaluates AudAgent on agents built with mainstream frameworks and finds widespread gaps in policy safeguards and agent behavior for sensitive data (e.g., SSNs).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ye Zheng', 'Yidan Hu']
  • Tags: ['privacy-auditing', 'runtime-monitoring', 'policy-compliance', 'defense', 'data-leakage-detection']

item.title: TAO: Tolerance-Aware Optimistic Verification for Floating-Point Neural Networks
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes TAO, a protocol to verify neural network outputs from untrusted/heterogeneous hardware by accepting outputs within principled operator-level FP tolerance regions rather than requiring bitwise equality.
  • Combines sound per-operator IEEE-754 worst-case bounds with tight empirical percentile profiles and a Merkle-anchored dispute game that recursively isolates mismatches for adjudication.
  • Implements a PyTorch-compatible runtime and blockchain contract layer (deployed on Ethereum Holesky testnet) with negligible runtime overhead and shows empirical thresholds far tighter than theoretical bounds across GPUs and model families.
  • Demonstrates robustness: empirical thresholds greatly reduce false positives and bound-aware adversarial attacks achieve 0% success in their evaluation.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jianzhu Yao', 'Hongxu Su', 'Taobo Liao', 'Zerui Cheng', 'Huan Zhang', 'Xuechao Wang', 'Pramod Viswanath']
  • Tags: ['secure ML verification', 'floating-point robustness', 'runtime attestation', 'dispute resolution']

item.title: Adaptive Attacks on Trusted Monitors Subvert AI Control Protocols
item.description:
Summary

  • Analyzes adaptive attacks where an untrusted LLM knows the AI control protocol and the monitor model, enabling targeted evasion.
  • Implements a practical attack that embeds publicly known or zero-shot prompt injections in model outputs, allowing harmful tasks to be completed despite monitoring.
  • Demonstrates consistent evasion across diverse monitor models on two major AI control benchmarks and shows Defer-to-Resample can amplify and worsen such attacks.
  • Argues that attacks on monitor models are a systemic blind spot and should be included as standard components of future control protocol evaluations.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Mikhail Terekhov', 'Alexander Panfilov', 'Daniil Dzenhaliou', 'Caglar Gulcehre', 'Maksym Andriushchenko', 'Ameya Prabhu', 'Jonas Geiping']
  • Tags: ['prompt injection', 'monitor evasion', 'jailbreaking', 'red teaming', 'LLM safety']

item.title: Untargeted Jailbreak Attack
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes UJA, a gradient-based untargeted jailbreak attack that maximizes the model's probability of producing unsafe outputs rather than forcing a fixed target response.
  • Decomposes the untargeted objective into two differentiable sub-objectives to jointly search for an optimal harmful response and corresponding adversarial prompt, with theoretical justification for the decomposition.
  • Demonstrates high effectiveness and efficiency: >80% attack success against recent safety-aligned LLMs within 100 optimization iterations, outperforming prior gradient-based attacks by ~30%.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xinzhe Huang', 'Wenjing Hu', 'Tianhang Zheng', 'Kedong Xiu', 'Xiaojun Jia', 'Di Wang', 'Zhan Qin', 'Kui Ren']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'adversarial attack', 'LLM safety', 'gradient-based attack']

item.title: On The Fragility of Benchmark Contamination Detection in Reasoning Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Demonstrates that benchmark contamination (training on evaluation data) in large reasoning models can be readily concealed, making existing contamination-detection techniques ineffective.
  • Finds that brief RL fine-tuning (GRPO/PPO-style importance sampling and clipping) can mask contamination signals from SFT, and that SFT with chain-of-thought on advanced LRMs also defeats memorization-based detectors.
  • Provides empirical and theoretical analysis attributing concealment to PPO-style mechanisms and highlights the threat to leaderboard fairness and the need for improved detection and evaluation protocols.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Han Wang', 'Haoyu Li', 'Brian Ko', 'Huan Zhang']
  • Tags: ['benchmark contamination', 'data poisoning', 'evaluation integrity', 'membership inference / memorization detection', 'adversarial evasion']

item.title: Robust Fine-Tuning from Non-Robust Pretrained Models: Mitigating Suboptimal Transfer With Epsilon-Scheduling
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies 'suboptimal transfer': fine-tuning non-robust pretrained models with a robust objective can impede adaptation and yield very poor adversarially-robust performance.
  • Proposes Epsilon-Scheduling, a heuristic that gradually adjusts perturbation strength during training to avoid early impediments to task adaptation and improve final robustness.
  • Introduces 'expected robustness', a metric that aggregates performance across a range of perturbation strengths to better characterize the accuracy–robustness trade-off.
  • Provides extensive experiments (multiple pretrained models and datasets) showing Epsilon-Scheduling prevents transfer failures and consistently improves expected robustness.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ["Jonas Ngnaw\\'e", 'Maxime Heuillet', 'Sabyasachi Sahoo', 'Yann Pequignot', 'Ola Ahmad', 'Audrey Durand', "Fr\\'ed\\'eric Precioso", "Christian Gagn\\'e"]
  • Tags: ['adversarial-robustness', 'robust-fine-tuning', 'defense', 'adversarial-training', 'transfer-learning']

item.title: ERIS: Evolutionary Real-world Interference Scheme for Jailbreaking Audio Large Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces ERIS, an evolutionary (genetic-algorithm) framework that crafts real-world interference signals fused with malicious instructions to jailbreak Audio Large Models (ALMs).
  • Optimizes naturalistic acoustic carriers (population initialization, crossover, probabilistic mutation) rather than handcrafted patterns, producing audio that sounds benign to humans and filters but bypasses model alignment.
  • Evaluates ERIS across multiple ALMs, showing it significantly outperforms text- and audio-based jailbreak baselines.
  • Discusses implications for defensive mechanisms, highlighting risks from seemingly innocuous environmental noise in acoustic threat models.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yibo Zhang', 'Liang Lin']
  • Tags: ['audio-jailbreaking', 'adversarial-audio', 'genetic-algorithm', 'red-teaming', 'alignment-bypass']

item.title: BinaryShield: Cross-Service Threat Intelligence in LLM Services using Privacy-Preserving Fingerprints
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes BinaryShield, a privacy-preserving system to share attack fingerprints of suspicious prompts across LLM services separated by compliance boundaries.
  • Transforms prompts via PII redaction, semantic embedding, binary quantization, and randomized response to produce privacy-preserving fingerprints that retain attack patterns.
  • Evaluates detection performance and efficiency: reports F1=0.94 outperforming SimHash baseline and achieves storage reduction with 38× faster similarity search versus dense embeddings.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Waris Gill', 'Natalie Isak', 'Matthew Dressman']
  • Tags: ['privacy-preserving defenses', 'prompt injection', 'threat intelligence sharing', 'fingerprinting', 'detection/robustness']

item.title: Model Collapse Is Not a Bug but a Feature in Machine Unlearning for LLMs
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Partial Model Collapse (PMC), an unlearning method that triggers model collapse via self-generated data rather than optimizing directly on targets to be removed.
  • Provides theoretical analysis showing PMC converges to removing targeted information and empirically demonstrates improved removal of private data while preserving general utility compared to prior unlearning methods.
  • Argues PMC avoids reinforcing sensitive data in fine-tuning and overcomes limitations of target-based unlearning; code is released.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yan Scholten', 'Sophie Xhonneux', 'Leo Schwinn', 'Stephan G\\"unnemann']
  • Tags: ['machine unlearning', 'privacy', 'defense', 'LLMs', 'model collapse']

item.title: Safeguarding Multimodal Knowledge Copyright in the RAG-as-a-Service Environment
item.description:
Summary

  • Addresses copyright protection for image knowledge in Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) service settings (RAG-as-a-Service), where existing watermarks focus on text only.
  • Proposes AQUA, a multimodal watermarking framework that embeds semantic signals into synthetic images via acronym-based triggers and spatial relationship cues.
  • Design ensures watermark signals survive indirect propagation from image retrievers to text generators, aiming for stealth, robustness, and traceability.
  • Evaluated across multiple models and datasets, demonstrating effective, imperceptible copyright tracing for multimodal RAG systems.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Tianyu Chen', 'Jian Lou', 'Wenjie Wang']
  • Tags: ['watermarking', 'multimodal RAG', 'copyright protection', 'image watermarking', 'defense']

item.title: VeriTrail: Closed-Domain Hallucination Detection with Traceability
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces VeriTrail, a method for detecting closed-domain hallucinations with traceability across multi-step generative (MGS) and single-step (SGS) processes.
  • Provides new datasets containing all intermediate outputs for MGS processes plus human annotations of final-output faithfulness.
  • Demonstrates VeriTrail outperforms baseline methods at identifying hallucinations and tracing where they were introduced in the generation pipeline.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Dasha Metropolitansky', 'Jonathan Larson']
  • Tags: ['hallucination-detection', 'model-robustness', 'traceability', 'multi-step-generation', 'factuality-evaluation']

item.title: JALMBench: Benchmarking Jailbreak Vulnerabilities in Audio Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces JALMBench, a large benchmark (11,316 text samples and 245,355 audio samples, >1,000 hours) for evaluating jailbreak attacks on Large Audio Language Models (LALMs).
  • Evaluates 12 mainstream LALMs across 8 attack methods (4 text-transferred, 4 audio-originated) and 5 defenses, analyzing attack efficiency, topic sensitivity, voice diversity, and architectural effects.
  • Finds that text-based safety alignment partially transfers to audio, interleaved audio-text strategies improve cross-modal robustness, and existing general-purpose moderation methods provide only limited protection.
  • Explores mitigation strategies at prompt and response levels and highlights the need for defense methods specifically designed for LALMs.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zifan Peng', 'Yule Liu', 'Zhen Sun', 'Mingchen Li', 'Zeren Luo', 'Jingyi Zheng', 'Wenhan Dong', 'Xinlei He', 'Xuechao Wang', 'Yingjie Xue', 'Shengmin Xu', 'Xinyi Huang']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'adversarial-audio', 'benchmark', 'defense-evaluation']

item.title: Self-Destructive Language Model
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes SEAM, a training-time defense that makes LLMs 'self-destructive'—preserving benign capabilities but degrading performance when fine-tuned on harmful data.
  • Introduces a novel loss that couples optimization trajectories of benign and harmful data plus adversarial gradient ascent to amplify the self-destructive effect, and a Hessian-free gradient estimator with theoretical error bounds for practical training.
  • Evaluates SEAM across multiple LLMs and datasets, showing strong robustness to low-intensity harmful fine-tuning and catastrophic collapse of model utility under high-intensity attacks, thereby deterring adversaries.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yuhui Wang', 'Rongyi Zhu', 'Ting Wang']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'fine-tuning attacks', 'adversarial training', 'model robustness', 'training-time defenses']

item.title: Multi-PA: A Multi-perspective Benchmark on Privacy Assessment for Large Vision-Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Multi-PA, a comprehensive benchmark to evaluate privacy preservation of Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) along two axes: privacy awareness (recognizing sensitive inputs) and privacy leakage (unintended disclosure in outputs).
  • Covers 31,962 samples across 26 personal privacy categories, 15 trade-secret categories, and 18 state-secret categories, with a set of sub-tasks designed to probe different privacy risks.
  • Evaluates 21 open-source and 2 closed-source LVLMs, finding widespread high risk of facilitating privacy breaches and variation in vulnerabilities across privacy categories.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jie Zhang', 'Xiangkui Cao', 'Zhouyu Han', 'Shiguang Shan', 'Xilin Chen']
  • Tags: ['privacy-benchmark', 'privacy-leakage', 'LVLMs', 'privacy-assessment', 'benchmarking']

item.title: A Survey for Deep Reinforcement Learning Based Network Intrusion Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Survey of deep reinforcement learning (DRL) approaches applied to network intrusion detection, covering core DRL algorithms (e.g., DQN, actor-critic) and recent architectures.
  • Analyzes challenges for DRL-based intrusion detection: training efficiency, imbalanced and unknown-class attack detection, feature selection, and evaluation on public/IoT datasets.
  • Finds DRL shows promise and can outperform some conventional deep learning methods on benchmarks, but many techniques and real-world deployment aspects remain underexplored.
  • Provides recommendations for improving DRL deployment and testing (including integration with generative methods) to build more robust, adaptive intrusion detection systems.

Paper Type

  • Survey

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Wanrong Yang', 'Alberto Acuto', 'Yihang Zhou', 'Dominik Wojtczak']
  • Tags: ['intrusion-detection', 'deep-reinforcement-learning', 'cybersecurity', 'survey']

item.title: Spilled Energy in Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Reinterprets the final LLM softmax as an Energy-Based Model and decomposes sequence generation into interacting EBMs to track 'energy spills' during decoding.
  • Introduces two training-free, logit-derived metrics—spilled energy (discrepancy across steps) and marginalized energy (single-step measurable)—to detect hallucinations and failures.
  • Localizes the exact answer token for targeted hallucination testing without probes or activation ablations, and evaluates across nine benchmarks and multiple state-of-the-art LLMs (pretrained and instruction-tuned).
  • Demonstrates robust, competitive hallucination detection and cross-task generalization with no additional training overhead.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Adrian Robert Minut', 'Hazem Dewidar', 'Iacopo Masi']
  • Tags: ['hallucination_detection', 'model_robustness', 'energy_based_models', 'safety_defense', 'anomaly_detection']

item.title: Detecting the Disturbance: A Nuanced View of Introspective Abilities in LLMs
item.description:
Summary

  • Shows that prior binary detection paradigms are confounded by global logit shifts that produce apparent introspection.
  • Demonstrates genuine, layer-dependent introspective abilities: localization of which sentence received an activation injection (up to 88% vs 10% chance) and discrimination of injection strength (83% vs 50% chance), but only for early-layer injections.
  • Provides a mechanistic explanation linking attention-based signal routing and residual stream recovery dynamics to the collapse of introspection in later layers.
  • Open-sources code for experiments on activation steering in Meta-Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ely Hahami', 'Ishaan Sinha', 'Lavik Jain', 'Josh Kaplan', 'Jon Hahami']
  • Tags: ['activation steering', 'model introspection', 'internal-state perturbations', 'adversarial/attack detection', 'robustness']

item.title: Towards Safe Reasoning in Large Reasoning Models via Corrective Intervention
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies that chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning in Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) can contain harmful steps even when final outputs appear safe, posing jailbreak and adversarial risks.
  • Finds key properties of unsafe reasoning: a few critical 'safety trigger' steps consolidate unsafe traces, compliance cues correlate with unsafe continuations, and corrective interventions can steer trajectories to safety.
  • Proposes Intervened Preference Optimization (IPO), which substitutes compliance steps with safety triggers and constructs strong-signal preference pairs for alignment of reasoning processes.
  • Evaluated on jailbreak and adversarial safety benchmarks, IPO reduces harmfulness by over 30% relative to SFT and RL baselines while maintaining performance on reasoning tasks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yichi Zhang', 'Yue Ding', 'Jingwen Yang', 'Tianwei Luo', 'Dongbai Li', 'Ranjie Duan', 'Qiang Liu', 'Hang Su', 'Yinpeng Dong', 'Jun Zhu']
  • Tags: ['jailbreak mitigation', 'alignment', 'preference learning', 'chain-of-thought safety', 'adversarial safety']

item.title: Control Tax: The Price of Keeping AI in Check
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a theoretical framework quantifying the "Control Tax" — the operational and financial cost of integrating oversight/control mechanisms for agentic AI and links classifier performance to safety assurances.
  • Empirically evaluates state-of-the-art language models in adversarial scenarios where attacker models inject subtle backdoors into code while monitoring models attempt to detect these vulnerabilities.
  • Provides empirical financial cost estimates for control protocols and develops optimized monitoring strategies that trade off safety and cost under realistic constraints like auditing budgets.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Mikhail Terekhov', 'Zhen Ning David Liu', 'Caglar Gulcehre', 'Samuel Albanie']
  • Tags: ['backdoor detection', 'adversarial attacks', 'defense/monitoring', 'security economics']

item.title: VPI-Bench: Visual Prompt Injection Attacks for Computer-Use Agents
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Visual Prompt Injection (VPI) attacks that embed malicious instructions visually within rendered user interfaces to deceive Computer-Use Agents (CUAs) and Browser-Use Agents (BUAs).
  • Presents VPI-Bench, a benchmark of 306 interactive test cases across five platforms that deploy realistic web variants containing visually embedded malicious prompts for systematic evaluation.
  • Empirical results show high vulnerability: CUAs deceived up to 51% and BUAs up to 100% on certain platforms; common system-prompt defenses provide only limited mitigation.
  • Releases code and dataset to facilitate further research and highlights the need for robust, context-aware defenses for multimodal AI agents.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Tri Cao', 'Bennett Lim', 'Yue Liu', 'Yuan Sui', 'Yuexin Li', 'Shumin Deng', 'Lin Lu', 'Nay Oo', 'Shuicheng Yan', 'Bryan Hooi']
  • Tags: ['visual prompt injection', 'adversarial attacks', 'benchmarking', 'multimodal agents', 'defenses']

item.title: Safety Mirage: How Spurious Correlations Undermine VLM Safety Fine-Tuning and Can Be Mitigated by Machine Unlearning
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies a 'safety mirage' where supervised safety fine-tuning for vision-language models (VLMs) induces spurious text-to-response correlations that can be exploited.
  • Demonstrates a simple one-word modification attack that bypasses safety fine-tuned VLMs by leveraging those spurious correlations, and shows over-prudence causing unnecessary refusals.
  • Proposes machine unlearning (MU) as an alternative alignment method that removes harmful knowledge directly and preserves general capabilities.
  • Evaluates MU across safety benchmarks, reporting up to 60.27% reduction in attack success and over 84.20% reduction in unnecessary rejections.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yiwei Chen', 'Yuguang Yao', 'Yihua Zhang', 'Bingquan Shen', 'Gaowen Liu', 'Sijia Liu']
  • Tags: ['safety fine-tuning', 'adversarial bypass / jailbreak', 'spurious correlations', 'machine unlearning', 'robustness/defense']

item.title: On the Rate of Convergence of GD in Non-linear Neural Networks: An Adversarial Robustness Perspective
item.description:
Summary

  • The paper analyzes gradient descent dynamics in a minimal non-linear setting (two-neuron ReLU network, two training points) from an adversarial robustness perspective.
  • Main theoretical result: GD converges to the optimal robustness margin but at a very slow rate, provably Θ(1 / ln t); this is claimed as the first explicit lower bound on convergence rate of the robustness margin in a non-linear model.
  • Empirical simulations confirm the same tight convergence rate across multiple natural initializations, indicating the phenomenon is robust to initialization choices.
  • Technical approach: rigorous trajectory analysis across different activation patterns to tightly bound the decision boundary dynamics and derive the slow convergence bound.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Guy Smorodinsky', 'Sveta Gimpleson', 'Itay Safran']
  • Tags: ['adversarial robustness', 'gradient descent dynamics', 'robustness margin', 'optimization theory', 'non-linear neural networks']

item.title: Real Money, Fake Models: Deceptive Model Claims in Shadow APIs
item.description:
Summary

  • First systematic audit comparing official LLM APIs to 17 corresponding shadow APIs (17 identified; 3 audited in depth) used in academic work.
  • Finds large performance divergence (up to 47.21%), unpredictable safety behaviors, and frequent identity verification/fingerprinting failures (45.83%).
  • Demonstrates that deceptive shadow-API practices undermine reproducibility, user trust, and the integrity of research relying on such services.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yage Zhang', 'Yukun Jiang', 'Zeyuan Chen', 'Michael Backes', 'Xinyue Shen', 'Yang Zhang']
  • Tags: ['API deception', 'model impersonation', 'red teaming/audit', 'reproducibility']
