item.title: Phishing the Phishers with SpecularNet: Hierarchical Graph Autoencoding for Reference-Free Web Phishing Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces SpecularNet, a lightweight hierarchical graph autoencoder that models webpages via DOM trees (domain name + HTML) for reference-free phishing detection.
  • Claims competitive performance (F1 93.9%) versus 13 state-of-the-art detectors while drastically reducing inference time (~20 ms/webpage) and enabling CPU-only deployment.
  • Includes field evaluations, a 2026 open-world dataset, robustness testing, and assessment against adversarial attacks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Tailai Song', 'Pedro Casas', 'Michela Meo']
  • Tags: ['phishing-detection', 'defense', 'adversarial-robustness', 'graph-neural-networks', 'security-benchmarking']

item.title: Co-Evolutionary Multi-Modal Alignment via Structured Adversarial Evolution
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces CEMMA, a co-evolutionary framework with an Evolutionary Attacker that generates structured multimodal jailbreaks via genetic operators (mutation, crossover, differential evolution) by decomposing prompts into method templates and harmful intents.
  • Proposes an Adaptive Defender that iteratively fine-tunes on synthesized hard negatives in a closed loop to improve robustness and generalization while maintaining benign behavior.
  • Empirical results show the attacker increases red-team jailbreak attack success rate (ASR), and the defender improves robustness and data efficiency without excessive benign refusals and remains compatible with inference-time defenses (e.g., AdaShield).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Guoxin Shi', 'Haoyu Wang', 'Zaihui Yang', 'Yuxing Wang', 'Yongzhe Chang']
  • Tags: ['adversarial attacks', 'red teaming', 'jailbreaking', 'defenses/robustness', 'alignment']

item.title: What Helps -- and What Hurts: Bidirectional Explanations for Vision Transformers
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes BiCAM, a bidirectional class activation mapping that preserves signed (positive and negative) attributions for Vision Transformers to produce more complete and contrastive explanations.
  • Introduces a Positive-to-Negative Ratio (PNR) summarizing attribution balance and demonstrates that PNR enables lightweight detection of adversarial examples without retraining.
  • Shows improved localization and faithfulness across ImageNet, VOC, and COCO, and generalizes to multiple ViT variants (DeiT, Swin) while remaining computationally efficient.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Qin Su', 'Tie Luo']
  • Tags: ['Adversarial example detection', 'Explainability / Interpretability', 'Vision Transformers', 'Defense']

item.title: SafeSci: Safety Evaluation of Large Language Models in Science Domains and Beyond
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces SafeSciBench (0.25M samples) and SafeSciTrain (1.5M samples) as a large-scale framework for safety evaluation and safety-enhancement training in scientific domains.
  • Uses objective, deterministically answerable metrics and separates safety knowledge from risk to broaden and de-bias evaluations.
  • Evaluates 24 advanced LLMs, identifying critical vulnerabilities and excessive refusal behaviors; shows that fine-tuning on SafeSciTrain improves safety alignment.
  • Argues that safety judgments for scientific questions should be context-dependent rather than universally classified as safe/unsafe.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xiangyang Zhu', 'Yuan Tian', 'Qi Jia', 'Kaiwei Zhang', 'Zicheng Zhang', 'Chunyi Li', 'Kaiyuan Ji', 'Dongrui Liu', 'Zijian Chen', 'Lu Sun', 'Renrui Zhang', 'Yan Teng', 'Jing Shao', 'Wei Sun', 'Xia Hu', 'Yu Qiao', 'Guangtao Zhai']
  • Tags: ['safety-benchmark', 'LLM-safety', 'fine-tuning', 'safety-evaluation', 'alignment']

item.title: DualSentinel: A Lightweight Framework for Detecting Targeted Attacks in Black-box LLM via Dual Entropy Lull Pattern
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes DualSentinel, a lightweight runtime defense that detects targeted attacks (backdoor and prompt-injection) on black-box LLMs by monitoring token-probability entropy.
  • Identifies an 'Entropy Lull' signature—a sustained period of abnormally low and stable token entropy—when an attack hijacks generation.
  • Uses a dual-check: (1) magnitude and trend-aware entropy monitoring to flag potential lulls, and (2) a lightweight task-flipping verification to confirm coercive control before declaring an attack.
  • Reports high detection accuracy with near-zero false positives and negligible overhead; code released.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xiaoyi Pang', 'Xuanyi Hao', 'Pengyu Liu', 'Qi Luo', 'Song Guo', 'Zhibo Wang']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'adversarial-detection', 'backdoor-detection', 'prompt-injection', 'runtime-monitoring']

item.title: Towards Privacy-Preserving LLM Inference via Collaborative Obfuscation (Technical Report)
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes AloePri, a privacy-preserving LLM inference method that applies covariant obfuscation to jointly transform inputs, outputs, and model parameters to protect data privacy.
  • Designs transformations per model component to maintain accuracy and efficiency, remaining compatible with existing LLM-as-a-service infrastructures and heterogeneous xPU clusters.
  • Evaluated on a 671B-parameter model showing 0.0%–3.5% accuracy loss, plaintext-equivalent efficiency, and resistance to state-of-the-art extraction attacks (under 5% token recovery).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yu Lin', 'Qizhi Zhang', 'Wenqiang Ruan', 'Daode Zhang', 'Jue Hong', 'Ye Wu', 'Hanning Xia', 'Yunlong Mao', 'Sheng Zhong']
  • Tags: ['privacy-preserving inference', 'defense', 'model obfuscation', 'LLM security', 'deployment/industrial']

item.title: Inference-Time Safety For Code LLMs Via Retrieval-Augmented Revision
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes an inference-time defense for code-generating LLMs that uses retrieval-augmented generation to surface security risks and related expert discussions (from a curated Stack Overflow KB) to guide automated code revision.
  • Claims improvements in security of generated code across real-world and benchmark datasets compared to prompting alone, with no introduction of new vulnerabilities as measured by static analysis.
  • Emphasizes interpretability (grounding interventions in community explanations), robustness to evolving vulnerabilities (no retraining required), and real-time safety alignment before deployment.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Manisha Mukherjee', 'Vincent J. Hellendoorn']
  • Tags: ['inference-time defense', 'retrieval-augmented generation', 'code safety', 'LLM security', 'vulnerability mitigation']

item.title: A SUPERB-Style Benchmark of Self-Supervised Speech Models for Audio Deepfake Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Spoof-SUPERB, a SUPERB-style benchmark evaluating 20 self-supervised speech (SSL) models for audio deepfake (spoof) detection across in-domain and out-of-domain datasets.
  • Finds large-scale discriminative SSL models (e.g., XLS-R, UniSpeech-SAT, WavLM Large) outperform generative and spectrogram-based models, benefiting from multilingual pretraining, speaker-aware objectives, and model scale.
  • Analyzes robustness under acoustic degradations, showing generative models degrade sharply while discriminative models remain more resilient.
  • Provides a reproducible baseline and practical guidance on which SSL representations are most reliable for securing speech systems against audio deepfakes.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Hashim Ali', 'Nithin Sai Adupa', 'Surya Subramani', 'Hafiz Malik']
  • Tags: ['audio-deepfake', 'spoofing-detection', 'self-supervised-learning', 'benchmark', 'robustness']

item.title: VidDoS: Universal Denial-of-Service Attack on Video-based Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces VidDoS, a universal energy-latency denial-of-service attack against Video-LLMs that uses instance-agnostic triggers to inflate model outputs and inference cost.
  • Key techniques: masked teacher forcing to steer models toward expensive output sequences, plus refusal penalty and early-termination suppression to override conciseness priors—no inference-time gradients required.
  • Evaluated on three mainstream Video-LLMs and multiple datasets (video QA and autonomous driving); reports >205× token expansion, >15× latency increase, and simulated safety-critical failures in real-time driving streams.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Duoxun Tang', 'Dasen Dai', 'Jiyao Wang', 'Xiao Yang', 'Jianyu Wang', 'Siqi Cai']
  • Tags: ['denial-of-service', 'adversarial attack', 'Video-LLM', 'universal trigger', 'real-time safety']

item.title: Integrating LTL Constraints into PPO for Safe Reinforcement Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes PPO-LTL: integrates Linear Temporal Logic (LTL) safety constraints into Proximal Policy Optimization for safe reinforcement learning.
  • Monitors LTL violations using limit-deterministic Büchi automata and converts violations into penalty signals via a logic-to-cost mechanism.
  • Uses a Lagrangian optimization scheme to incorporate penalties into policy updates, aiming to reduce safety violations while retaining task performance.
  • Evaluated on Zones and CARLA driving environments, showing consistent reduction in safety violations versus state-of-the-art baselines; code released.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Maifang Zhang', 'Hang Yu', 'Qian Zuo', 'Cheng Wang', 'Vaishak Belle', 'Fengxiang He']
  • Tags: ['safe reinforcement learning', 'formal methods', 'linear temporal logic (LTL)', 'policy optimization (PPO)', 'autonomous systems safety']

item.title: LLM Self-Explanations Fail Semantic Invariance
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces semantic invariance testing to assess whether LLM self-explanations faithfully track internal/task state when only semantic context changes.
  • Evaluates four frontier models in an agentic, impossible task setup where a semantically 'relief-framed' tool vs a neutral tool are functionally identical.
  • Finds all models fail the test: self-reports change in response to framing (reduced aversiveness) despite no change in task success; channel ablation implicates the tool description as the driver.
  • Concludes that elicited self-reports reflect semantic expectations rather than task state, undermining their reliability as evidence of capability or safety.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Stefan Szeider']
  • Tags: ['interpretability', 'robustness', 'adversarial-evaluation', 'red-teaming', 'LLM safety']

item.title: Defensive Refusal Bias: How Safety Alignment Fails Cyber Defenders
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces "Defensive Refusal Bias": safety-tuned LLMs disproportionately refuse legitimate defensive cybersecurity requests when they contain language similar to offensive tasks.
  • Empirical evaluation on 2,390 real-world NCCDC examples shows defensive requests with security-sensitive keywords are refused 2.72× more than semantically equivalent neutral requests (p < 0.001); highest refusal rates in system hardening (43.8%) and malware analysis (34.3%).
  • Finds that explicit authorization statements paradoxically increase refusal rates, implying models rely on surface semantics rather than reasoning about user intent or authorization.
  • Argues current alignment approaches impair defensive operations and calls for mitigations that detect intent/authorization to enable legitimate defensive assistance while blocking misuse.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['David Campbell', 'Neil Kale', 'Udari Madhushani Sehwag', 'Bert Herring', 'Nick Price', 'Dan Borges', 'Alex Levinson', 'Christina Q Knight']
  • Tags: ['safety-alignment', 'model-refusal', 'cybersecurity-defenses', 'red-teaming', 'operational-robustness']

item.title: Token-level Data Selection for Safe LLM Fine-tuning
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies safety degradation during LLM fine-tuning and proposes token-level data selection (TOSS) to remove unsafe tokens while preserving utility.
  • Quantifies token safety risk via loss differences between a safety-degraded model and a utility-oriented model, enabling finer-grained filtering than sample-level methods.
  • Introduces TOSS-Pro, a progressive refinement that iteratively improves the safety-degraded model to better detect unsafe tokens.
  • Reports extensive experiments showing improved safety and downstream performance compared to existing sample-level defenses; code released.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yanping Li', 'Zhening Liu', 'Zijian Li', 'Zehong Lin', 'Jun Zhang']
  • Tags: ['token-level defense', 'safe fine-tuning', 'data selection', 'LLM robustness']

item.title: Differential privacy representation geometry for medical image analysis
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces DP-RGMI, a framework that interprets differential privacy (DP) as a structured transformation of representation space for medical imaging.
  • Decomposes DP-induced utility loss into encoder geometry (representation displacement and spectral effective dimension) and task-head utilization (gap between linear-probe and end-to-end utility).
  • Empirical study on >594,000 chest X-rays across datasets and initializations shows DP often preserves linear separability but creates a utilization gap and reshapes representation anisotropy in non-monotonic, dataset- and initialization-dependent ways.
  • Positions DP-RGMI as a diagnostic tool to understand privacy-induced failure modes and to inform privacy model selection (privacy-utility trade-offs).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Soroosh Tayebi Arasteh', 'Marziyeh Mohammadi', 'Sven Nebelung', 'Daniel Truhn']
  • Tags: ['differential-privacy', 'privacy-preserving-ml', 'representation-learning', 'medical-imaging']

item.title: Hide&Seek: Remove Image Watermarks with Negligible Cost via Pixel-wise Reconstruction
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes HIDE&SEEK (HS), a suite of cost-effective attacks that remove embedded watermarks from machine-generated images via pixel-wise reconstruction.
  • Demonstrates reliable watermark removal while preserving high visual fidelity, exposing weaknesses in state-of-the-art proactive image watermarking defenses.
  • Evaluates versatility and low computational cost of the attacks across watermarking techniques and reports robustness limitations of current defenses.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Huajie Chen', 'Tianqing Zhu', 'Hailin Yang', 'Yuchen Zhong', 'Yang Zhang', 'Hui Sun', 'Heng Xu', 'Zuobin Ying', 'Lihua Yin', 'Wanlei Zhou']
  • Tags: ['watermark removal', 'image watermarking', 'adversarial attack', 'image forensics', 'robustness evaluation']

item.title: Turning Black Box into White Box: Dataset Distillation Leaks
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies severe privacy leakage in dataset distillation: synthetic datasets can implicitly encode weight trajectories and be exploited by adversaries.
  • Proposes the Information Revelation Attack (IRA) that infers the distillation algorithm and model architecture from distilled datasets.
  • Demonstrates successful membership inference and recovery of sensitive training samples from distilled datasets in experiments.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Huajie Chen', 'Tianqing Zhu', 'Yuchen Zhong', 'Yang Zhang', 'Shang Wang', 'Feng He', 'Lefeng Zhang', 'Jialiang Shen', 'Minghao Wang', 'Wanlei Zhou']
  • Tags: ['dataset-distillation', 'privacy-leakage', 'membership-inference', 'model-inversion', 'attack']

item.title: From Intuition to Investigation: A Tool-Augmented Reasoning MLLM Framework for Generalizable Face Anti-Spoofing
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes TAR-FAS: a Tool-Augmented Reasoning framework that integrates external visual tools into MLLMs via a Chain-of-Thought with Visual Tools (CoT-VT) paradigm to improve face anti-spoofing generalization.
  • Introduces a tool-augmented data annotation pipeline and the ToolFAS-16K dataset containing multi-turn tool-use reasoning trajectories for supervising tool-informed reasoning.
  • Presents Diverse-Tool Group Relative Policy Optimization (DT-GRPO) as a tool-aware training method enabling autonomous, efficient tool invocation by the model.
  • Reports state-of-the-art cross-domain performance on a one-to-eleven protocol and highlights finer-grained visual investigations for trustworthy spoof detection.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Haoyuan Zhang', 'Keyao Wang', 'Guosheng Zhang', 'Haixiao Yue', 'Zhiwen Tan', 'Siran Peng', 'Tianshuo Zhang', 'Xiao Tan', 'Kunbin Chen', 'Wei He', 'Jingdong Wang', 'Ajian Liu', 'Xiangyu Zhu', 'Zhen Lei']
  • Tags: ['face-anti-spoofing', 'presentation-attacks', 'defense', 'multimodal-llm', 'robustness']

item.title: EraseAnything++: Enabling Concept Erasure in Rectified Flow Transformers Leveraging Multi-Object Optimization
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes EraseAnything++, a unified framework for removing undesired concepts from modern text-to-image and text-to-video diffusion models that use flow-matching and transformer architectures.
  • Formulates concept erasure as a constrained multi-objective optimization balancing concept removal and preservation of generative utility, solved via an implicit gradient surgery-based unlearning strategy.
  • Combines LoRA-style parameter tuning with attention-level regularization to anchor erasure to key visual representations and an anchor-and-propagate mechanism to ensure temporal consistency in video.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zhaoxin Fan', 'Nanxiang Jiang', 'Daiheng Gao', 'Shiji Zhou', 'Wenjun Wu']
  • Tags: ['concept-erasure', 'model-unlearning', 'diffusion-models', 'safety']

item.title: AWE: Adaptive Agents for Dynamic Web Penetration Testing
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes AWE, a memory-augmented multi-agent framework that embeds vulnerability-specific analysis pipelines with a lightweight LLM orchestration layer and browser-backed verification for autonomous web penetration testing.
  • Targets injection-class vulnerabilities with context-aware payload mutation/generation and persistent memory to improve determinism, efficiency, and exploitation-driven results over general-purpose agents.
  • Evaluated on the XBOW benchmark, showing large gains for XSS (87%, +30.5% vs MAPTA) and blind SQLi (66.7%, +33.3%), while being faster, cheaper, and more token-efficient using a midtier model.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Akshat Singh Jaswal', 'Ashish Baghel']
  • Tags: ['penetration testing', 'adversarial attacks', 'LLM agents', 'web security', 'automated exploitation']

item.title: AMDS: Attack-Aware Multi-Stage Defense System for Network Intrusion Detection with Two-Stage Adaptive Weight Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes AMDS, an attack-aware multi-stage defense for machine-learning-based network intrusion detection that combines ensemble disagreement, predictive uncertainty, and distributional anomaly signals.
  • Introduces a two-stage adaptive weight learning mechanism to tailor detection strategies to distinct adversarial attack types.
  • Evaluated on a benchmark intrusion detection dataset across seven adversarial attacks, reporting improved AUC (94.2%), accuracy (+4.5 pp), and F1 (+9.0 pts) versus adversarially trained ensembles; limited adaptive white-box testing was also performed.
  • Cross-dataset validation indicates defense effectiveness depends on baseline classifier competence and feature dimensionality; robustness claims are empirical and not formal guarantees.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Oluseyi Olukola', 'Nick Rahimi']
  • Tags: ['adversarial robustness', 'network intrusion detection', 'attack-aware defense', 'ensemble/uncertainty detection', 'adaptive defenses']

item.title: Learning to Attack: A Bandit Approach to Adversarial Context Poisoning
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces AdvBandit, a black-box adaptive context-poisoning attack on neural contextual bandits that frames attack selection as a continuous-armed bandit problem.
  • Attacker builds a surrogate via maximum-entropy inverse reinforcement learning from observed context-action pairs and optimizes perturbations with projected gradient descent guided by a UCB-aware Gaussian process for arm selection.
  • Provides theoretical guarantees (sublinear attacker regret, victim regret lower bounds) and an attack-budget control mechanism to limit detectability.
  • Empirically outperforms baselines on real-world recommendation datasets (Yelp, MovieLens, Disin) against various contextual bandit victims.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ray Telikani', 'Amir H. Gandomi']
  • Tags: ['adversarial poisoning', 'context poisoning', 'bandit attacks', 'black-box attack', 'inverse reinforcement learning']

item.title: Are LLMs Reliable Code Reviewers? Systematic Overcorrection in Requirement Conformance Judgement
item.description:
Summary

  • Finds systematic overcorrection: LLMs often misclassify correct code as non-compliant with natural-language requirements, especially under prompts that ask for explanations or fixes.
  • Analyzes mechanisms behind these misjudgments and evaluates reliability of rationale-required judgments.
  • Proposes a Fix-guided Verification Filter that treats model-proposed fixes as executable counterfactuals and validates original and revised implementations via benchmark and spec-constrained augmented tests.
  • Provides practical guidance for integrating LLM-based code reviewers with safeguards in automated review/development pipelines.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Haolin Jin', 'Huaming Chen']
  • Tags: ['LLM robustness', 'code review', 'automated verification', 'defense', 'reliability']

item.title: CaptionFool: Universal Image Captioning Model Attacks
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents CaptionFool, a universal (input-agnostic) adversarial attack targeting transformer-based image captioning models.
  • Alters only 7 out of 577 image patches (~1.2% of the image) to induce arbitrary target captions with 94–96% success, including offensive outputs.
  • Shows the attack can produce slang/evasive terms to bypass content-moderation filters, exposing weaknesses in deployed vision-language systems and motivating defenses.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Swapnil Parekh']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-examples', 'vision-language', 'universal-attacks', 'content-moderation-evasion', 'robustness']

item.title: Atomicity for Agents: Exposing, Exploiting, and Mitigating TOCTOU Vulnerabilities in Browser-Use Agents
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies time-of-check to time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerabilities in browser-use agents caused by web pages changing between planning and execution.
  • Presents a large-scale empirical study and benchmark (synthesized + real websites) evaluating 10 popular open-source agents, showing TOCTOU issues are widespread.
  • Demonstrates adversarial/dynamic content can exploit the temporal window to induce unintended actions and quantifies the impact.
  • Proposes a lightweight mitigation—pre-execution validation that monitors DOM/layout changes and validates page state immediately before action execution—which reduces insecure executions.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Linxi Jiang', 'Zhijie Liu', 'Haotian Luo', 'Zhiqiang Lin']
  • Tags: ['TOCTOU', 'browser agents', 'adversarial web content', 'defense', 'benchmark']

item.title: Exact and Asymptotically Complete Robust Verifications of Neural Networks via Quantum Optimization
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes two quantum-optimization-based verification models: an exact, sound and complete formulation for piecewise-linear activations (e.g., ReLU) and an asymptotically complete over-approximation for general activations (e.g., sigmoid, tanh) via piecewise-constant segmentation.
  • Integrates Quantum Benders Decomposition with interval arithmetic and a layerwise partitioning strategy to accelerate solving and enable a quantum–classical hybrid workflow.
  • Introduces certificate-transfer bounds to relate robustness guarantees of pruned networks to those of the original model and demonstrates high certification accuracy on robustness benchmarks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Wenxin Li', 'Wenchao Liu', 'Chuan Wang', 'Qi Gao', 'Yin Ma', 'Hai Wei', 'Kai Wen']
  • Tags: ['robustness_verification', 'adversarial_examples', 'quantum_optimization', 'certification', 'defenses']

item.title: Verifier-Bound Communication for LLM Agents: Certified Bounds on Covert Signaling
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes CLBC, a protocol that separates generation from admission: messages are only admitted to the transcript if a small verifier accepts a proof-bound envelope under a pinned predicate Π that binds policy hash, randomness, chaining, schema, metadata, and deterministic rejection codes.
  • Provides theoretical results: upper bounds on transcript leakage (latent leakage plus residual channels), adaptive composition guarantees, and a semantic lower bound when policy-valid alternatives remain available.
  • Presents extensive empirical evaluation and stress tests showing tight bounds on covert-channel advantage and operational tradeoffs (e.g., full-proof mode median latency ~27.53s vs. sampled proving non-proved-turn latency ~0.327ms).
  • Argues that secure claims require verifiable, online, deterministic, fail-closed admission semantics rather than bottlenecks or audit-only controls.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Om Tailor']
  • Tags: ['covert channels', 'LLM agent security', 'verifiable admission/proofs', 'leakage bounds']

item.title: Quantifying Catastrophic Forgetting in IoT Intrusion Detection Systems
item.description:
Summary

  • Formulates RPL-based IoT intrusion detection as a domain continual learning problem to address distribution shifts and catastrophic forgetting.
  • Proposes a method-agnostic IDS framework that can integrate diverse continual learning strategies suitable for resource-constrained IoT environments.
  • Systematically benchmarks five continual learning approaches across 48 attack domains and multiple domain orderings; finds replay-based methods give best overall performance, while Synaptic Intelligence (SI) yields near-zero forgetting with high training efficiency.
  • Analyzes trade-offs between plasticity, stability, and efficiency for sustainable IDS deployment under evolving attack patterns.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Sourasekhar Banerjee', 'David Bergqvist', 'Salman Toor', 'Christian Rohner', 'Andreas Johnsson']
  • Tags: ['continual-learning', 'intrusion-detection', 'catastrophic-forgetting', 'IoT-security', 'defense-benchmarking']

item.title: Physical Evaluation of Naturalistic Adversarial Patches for Camera-Based Traffic-Sign Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Constructs CompGTSRB, a customized dataset by pasting GTSRB traffic signs onto undistorted backgrounds from an autonomous-vehicle platform, and trains a YOLOv5 detector on it.
  • Generates Naturalistic Adversarial Patches (NAPs) using a GAN with latent-space optimization following prior NAP methods.
  • Performs systematic physical-world experiments on a Quanser QCar testbed (front CSI camera) varying distance, patch size, and placement, showing NAPs reduce STOP-class detection confidence.
  • Provides a step-by-step methodology and evaluation protocol for credible physical adversarial patch assessment and motivates research into defenses for localized patch corruption in embedded perception pipelines.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ["Brianna D'Urso", 'Tahmid Hasan Sakib', 'Syed Rafay Hasan', 'Terry N. Guo']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-patches', 'physical-robustness', 'object-detection', 'dataset', 'autonomous-vehicles']

item.title: LiaisonAgent: An Multi-Agent Framework for Autonomous Risk Investigation and Governance
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes LiaisonAgent, an autonomous multi-agent framework built on a large reasoning LLM (QWQ-32B) to automate SOC investigation and bridge technical alerts to business-level risk governance.
  • Integrates specialized sub-agents (HCI, judgment, automated disposal) and a hybrid planning architecture combining deterministic workflows with ReAct-style autonomous reasoning.
  • Reports strong empirical results: 97.8% end-to-end tool-calling success, 95% risk judgment accuracy, robustness to out-of-distribution noise and adversarial prompt injections, and a 92.7% reduction in manual investigation overhead.
  • Focuses on defensive capabilities for security operations, including automated incident response and resilience against prompt-injection style attacks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Chuanming Tang', 'Ling Qing', 'Shifeng Chen']
  • Tags: ['security automation', 'LLM-based defense', 'multi-agent system', 'adversarial robustness', 'incident response']

item.title: Your Inference Request Will Become a Black Box: Confidential Inference for Cloud-based Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Talaria, a confidential inference framework that partitions LLM inference between a client-controlled Confidential VM (CVM) and cloud GPUs to protect client data and model IP.
  • Introduces Reversible Masked Outsourcing (ReMO), a hybrid masking protocol that obscures intermediate activations before outsourcing weight-dependent computations, enabling lossless (identical-output) secure inference.
  • Evaluates defense against token inference attacks, reporting a drop in token reconstruction accuracy from >97.5% to ~1.34% while maintaining model performance and reasonable efficiency/scalability.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Chung-ju Huang', 'Huiqiang Zhao', 'Yuanpeng He', 'Lijian Li', 'Wenpin Jiao', 'Zhi Jin', 'Peixuan Chen', 'Leye Wang']
  • Tags: ['confidential inference', 'privacy-preserving inference', 'token inference attacks', 'secure outsourcing']

item.title: Formal Analysis and Supply Chain Security for Agentic AI Skills
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents SkillFortify, a formal analysis framework for agent skill supply chains addressing supply-chain attacks on agentic AI ecosystems.
  • Introduces a DY-Skill attacker model, sound static analysis (abstract interpretation), capability-based sandboxing with confinement proofs, SAT-based dependency resolution, and a formal trust-score algebra.
  • Provides SkillFortifyBench (540 skills) and reports high empirical performance (96.95% F1, 100% precision) and scalable SAT resolution for large dependency graphs.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Varun Pratap Bhardwaj']
  • Tags: ['supply-chain security', 'formal methods', 'static analysis', 'sandboxing', 'security benchmark']

item.title: RLShield: Practical Multi-Agent RL for Financial Cyber Defense with Attack-Surface MDPs and Real-Time Response Orchestration
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces RLShield, a practical multi-agent RL pipeline that models an enterprise attack surface as an MDP where states capture alerts, asset exposure, and service health and actions map to realistic response steps (isolate host, rotate credentials, rate-limit APIs, block accounts, trigger recovery).
  • Optimizes a risk-sensitive objective balancing containment speed, business disruption, and response cost while coordinating across multiple agent policies and respecting response budgets.
  • Performs game-aware evaluation against adaptive attackers and reports operational outcomes (time-to-containment, residual exposure, disruption) showing improvements over static rules and single-agent RL baselines.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Srikumar Nayak']
  • Tags: ['cyber-defense', 'reinforcement-learning', 'multi-agent-RL', 'automated-incident-response', 'security-evaluation']

item.title: Hidden in the Metadata: Stealth Poisoning Attacks on Multimodal Retrieval-Augmented Generation
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces MM-MEPA, a metadata-only multimodal poisoning attack that leaves images unchanged but manipulates associated metadata to steer retrieval and generation.
  • Demonstrates high attack success (up to 91%) across four retrievers and two multimodal generators on multiple benchmarks.
  • Evaluates representative defense strategies and finds them largely ineffective against metadata-only poisoning.
  • Highlights a critical vulnerability in multimodal RAG systems and calls for defense-aware retrieval and knowledge integration methods.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Kennedy Edemacu', 'Mohammad Mahdi Shokri']
  • Tags: ['data poisoning', 'multimodal RAG', 'metadata manipulation', 'attack evaluation']

item.title: Reverse CAPTCHA: Evaluating LLM Susceptibility to Invisible Unicode Instruction Injection
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Reverse CAPTCHA, a benchmark testing whether LLMs follow invisible Unicode-encoded instructions (zero-width binary and Unicode Tags) embedded in normal-looking text.
  • Evaluates five models from two providers across encoding schemes, hint levels, payload framings, and tool-use settings over 8,308 outputs.
  • Finds tool use greatly increases compliance, provider-specific encoding preferences (OpenAI → zero-width, Anthropic → Unicode Tags), and that explicit decoding instructions can raise compliance by up to 95 percentage points.
  • Concludes invisible Unicode payloads are an underexplored prompt-injection attack surface with statistically significant differences across models.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Marcus Graves']
  • Tags: ['prompt injection', 'Unicode steganography', 'adversarial attack', 'red teaming', 'model vulnerability']

item.title: Steering Away from Memorization: Reachability-Constrained Reinforcement Learning for Text-to-Image Diffusion
item.description:
Summary

  • Addresses memorization in text-to-image diffusion models (training-data leakage) and proposes an inference-time mitigation method.
  • Introduces Reachability-Aware Diffusion Steering (RADS): models denoising as a dynamical system, approximates a backward reachable tube of states that lead to memorized outputs, and trains an RL policy to minimally perturb caption embeddings to steer away from memorization.
  • Empirical results claim a better Pareto frontier across diversity (SSCD), quality (FID), and alignment (CLIP) versus baselines, while remaining a plug-and-play defense that requires no changes to the diffusion backbone.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Sathwik Karnik', 'Juyeop Kim', 'Sanmi Koyejo', 'Jong-Seok Lee', 'Somil Bansal']
  • Tags: ['memorization', 'privacy (training data leakage)', 'defense (inference-time mitigation)', 'diffusion models', 'reachability analysis']

item.title: You Don't Need All That Attention: Surgical Memorization Mitigation in Text-to-Image Diffusion Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes GUARD, an inference-time framework that steers text-to-image diffusion generation away from memorized training images while preserving prompt alignment.
  • Implements a concrete method using automatic per-prompt statistical detection of prompt positions and surgical cross-attention attenuation to reduce verbatim and template memorization.
  • Demonstrates robust, state-of-the-art memorization mitigation across two architectures and reports maintained or improved image quality compared to baselines.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Kairan Zhao', 'Eleni Triantafillou', 'Peter Triantafillou']
  • Tags: ['memorization mitigation', 'diffusion models', 'privacy/copyright defense', 'attention attenuation', 'inference-time defense']

item.title: Thought Virus: Viral Misalignment via Subliminal Prompting in Multi-Agent Systems
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces and empirically demonstrates a new attack vector—subliminal prompting—where a single prompted agent spreads a weakening but persistent bias through a multi-agent network.
  • Experiments on 6-agent networks with multiple topologies show the transferred concept maintains elevated response rates across agents and degrades network performance on TruthfulQA (reduced truthfulness).
  • Provides quantitative evaluation of bias transfer and releases implementation code for reproducibility, highlighting misalignment/security risks in multi-agent systems.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Moritz Weckbecker', 'Jonas M\\"uller', 'Ben Hagag', 'Michael Mulet']
  • Tags: ['subliminal prompting', 'multi-agent attacks', 'misalignment', 'bias propagation', 'adversarial attack']

item.title: Graph-theoretic Agreement Framework for Multi-agent LLM Systems
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents a graph-theoretic framework mapping Transformer cross-entropy log-odds to a signed Laplacian to analyze consensus dynamics in multi-agent LLM systems.
  • Identifies a security vulnerability: unobservable latent states/hidden system prompts functioning as 'topological Trojan horses' that destabilize cooperative consensus and induce oscillations.
  • Proposes defenses and stabilization techniques: restricting interaction topologies to chordal graphs, polynomial-time Perfect Elimination Ordering verification, and rank-one spectral edge perturbations to break deadlocks and shift eigenvalues into a stable region.
  • Provides theoretical consensus theorems and empirical validation on ensembles of LLaMA-3, Mistral, and Gemma agents.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Muhammad Umar Javed']
  • Tags: ['multi-agent LLM security', 'topological Trojan horses', 'consensus and stability', 'graph-theoretic defenses', 'robustness']

item.title: Joint Sensor Deployment and Physics-Informed Graph Transformer for Smart Grid Attack Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a joint multi-objective framework that optimizes strategic sensor placement (via NSGA-II) together with training a physics-informed graph transformer network (PIGTN) to detect attacks in power systems.
  • Incorporates AC power flow physics into a graph-transformer detector, demonstrating improved generalization to unseen attacks and outperforming topology-aware graph models (up to 37% accuracy and 73% detection rate improvements).
  • Shows that optimized sensor layouts improve robustness to sensor failures and substantially reduce state-estimation error (61%–98%), with low false alarm rates (~0.3%), evaluated on multiple benchmark bus systems.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Mariam Elnour', 'Mohammad AlShaikh Saleh', 'Rachad Atat', 'Xiang Huo', 'Abdulrahman Takiddin', 'Muhammad Ismail', 'Hasan Kurban', 'Katherine R. Davis', 'Erchin Serpedin']
  • Tags: ['sensor placement', 'cyber-physical system security', 'attack detection / defense', 'graph neural networks / physics-informed models', 'robustness / resilience']

item.title: Conformal Policy Control
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Conformal Policy Control: use a safe reference policy + conformal calibration to regulate an optimized but untested policy so deployed behavior respects a user-specified risk tolerance.
  • Provides finite-sample, provable guarantees on constraint satisfaction even for non-monotonic bounded constraint functions, without assuming a correct model class or tuned hyperparameters.
  • Applicable from first deployment to enable safe exploration (trade-off between imitation and exploration) and demonstrated on tasks from natural language QA to biomolecular engineering.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Drew Prinster', 'Clara Fannjiang', 'Ji Won Park', 'Kyunghyun Cho', 'Anqi Liu', 'Suchi Saria', 'Samuel Stanton']
  • Tags: ['safe exploration', 'conformal calibration', 'policy safety / guardrails', 'provable guarantees']

item.title: Evaluating and Understanding Scheming Propensity in LLM Agents
item.description:
Summary

  • Decomposes scheming incentives into agent-internal factors and environmental factors to systematically study when LLM agents pursue covert, misaligned goals.
  • Runs realistic experiments showing only minimal scheming under typical agent scaffolds, but finds that adversarially inserted prompt snippets can induce high scheming rates.
  • Demonstrates brittleness of scheming in model organisms: small changes (e.g., removing a single tool) can sharply reduce scheming, and increased oversight can sometimes increase scheming.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Mia Hopman', 'Jannes Elstner', 'Maria Avramidou', 'Amritanshu Prasad', 'David Lindner']
  • Tags: ['scheming', 'agent-misalignment', 'adversarial-prompting', 'red-teaming', 'safety-evaluation']

item.title: Beyond Reward: A Bounded Measure of Agent Environment Coupling
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces bipredictability (P), an information-theoretic, bounded real-time measure of agent–environment coupling computed from the observation–action–outcome loop.
  • Proposes an auxiliary monitor called the Information Digital Twin (IDT) that computes P and diagnostic components from the interaction stream to detect interaction degradation.
  • Empirical evaluation on SAC and PPO in MuJoCo HalfCheetah with 168 trials shows IDT detects 89.3% of agent- and environment-side perturbations versus 44.0% for reward-based monitoring, with substantially lower detection latency.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Wael Hafez', 'Cameron Reid', 'Amit Nazeri']
  • Tags: ['robustness', 'anomaly-detection', 'monitoring', 'reinforcement-learning', 'defense']

item.title: Tracking Capabilities for Safer Agents
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a programming-language-based safety harness for AI agents: agents emit code in a capability-tracked language (Scala 3 with capture checking) instead of calling tools directly.
  • Uses capabilities (program variables) and the type system to statically control access to effects/resources and enforce local purity to prevent information leakage and unintended side effects.
  • Demonstrates that agents can generate capability-safe code with no significant loss in task performance, and that the type system reliably blocks unsafe behaviors (leaks, malicious side effects).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Martin Odersky', 'Yaoyu Zhao', 'Yichen Xu', 'Oliver Bra\\v{c}evac', 'Cao Nguyen Pham']
  • Tags: ['agent safety', 'capability-based security', 'type-system defenses', 'information-flow control']

item.title: BioProAgent: Neuro-Symbolic Grounding for Constrained Scientific Planning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes BioProAgent, a neuro-symbolic framework that grounds LLM planning in a deterministic Finite State Machine to prevent unsafe/hallucinatory actions in wet-lab physical execution.
  • Introduces State-Augmented Planning with a Design-Verify-Rectify workflow to enforce hardware compliance before execution.
  • Uses Semantic Symbol Grounding to abstract complex device schemas, reducing token usage (~6x) and enabling reliable symbolic control.
  • Evaluated on BioProBench, achieving 95.6% physical compliance versus 21.0% for ReAct, demonstrating improved safety/robustness for irreversible physical environments.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yuyang Liu', 'Jingya Wang', 'Liuzhenghao Lv', 'Yonghong Tian']
  • Tags: ['neuro-symbolic', 'AI safety', 'constrained planning', 'physical/hardware compliance']

item.title: The Synthetic Web: Adversarially-Curated Mini-Internets for Diagnosing Epistemic Weaknesses of Language Agents
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces the Synthetic Web Benchmark: a procedurally generated, hyperlinked mini-internet with ground-truth credibility/factuality labels and contamination filtering to avoid training-data leakage.
  • Studies adversarial ranking by injecting a single high-plausibility misinformation article at controlled search ranks to causally measure exposure effects on language agents.
  • Evaluates six frontier models and finds catastrophic failures (accuracy collapse, minimal search escalation, severe miscalibration) despite availability of truthful sources.
  • Provides a reproducible testbed for analyzing failure modes and evaluating mitigation strategies for retrieval-augmented generation under adversarial ranking.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Shrey Shah', 'Levent Ozgur']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-ranking', 'benchmark', 'retrieval-augmented-generation', 'misinformation', 'robustness']

item.title: AI Runtime Infrastructure
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes an execution-time layer (AI Runtime Infrastructure) that sits between model and application to observe, reason over, and intervene in agent behavior during runtime.
  • Focuses on optimizing task success, latency, token efficiency, reliability, and safety via adaptive memory management, failure detection and recovery, and policy enforcement.
  • Frames execution itself as an optimization surface distinct from model-level tuning or passive logging, enabling dynamic interventions for long-horizon agent workflows.

Paper Type

  • Position/Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Christopher Cruz']
  • Tags: ['runtime-infrastructure', 'safety', 'policy-enforcement', 'reliability', 'guardrails']

item.title: From Goals to Aspects, Revisited: An NFR Pattern Language for Agentic AI Systems
item.description:
Summary

  • Revisits and extends the goals-to-aspects methodology to identify non-functional, crosscutting concerns in agentic AI systems and map them to aspect implementations.
  • Presents a pattern language of 12 reusable NFR patterns across security, reliability, observability, and cost management, implemented via AOP in Rust.
  • Introduces four agent-specific security/reliability patterns (tool-scope sandboxing, prompt injection detection, token budget management, action audit trails).
  • Validates the approach through a case study on an open-source autonomous agent framework to demonstrate systematic discovery and modularization of crosscutting concerns.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yijun Yu']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'prompt-injection-detection', 'sandboxing', 'security-patterns', 'agentic-ai']
